finders.dimension: Best-Practices Conformance (Claude Code hook semantics vs. current official docs + anthropics/claude-code issues)
finders.summary: Cross-referenced the live board's hook-bearing items and the staged remediation artifacts against the CURRENT official Claude Code hooks reference (code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks) and anthropics/claude-code issues. The prior reconciliation pass left a class of CLAIM≠REALITY divergences in the hook-mechanics items that will mislead an executor and silently disarm the very anti-loop / steering behaviors the spine exists to provide. Five substantive findings. P0: the load-bearing E8-13 settings-registration AC (seq 211) was NEVER reconciled to the prior pass's own as-scoped decision — its name + description still say "six-hook ... SessionStart, PreCompact" with matcher "Write|Edit|MultiEdit", while the staged 01-settings.json the same pass authored registers only 5 events (PreCompact deliberately deferred to NEW-11) and uses matcher "Edit|Write|MultiEdit|Bash"; an executor following the board AC re-registers a non-existent PreCompact hook and drops the Bash leg that closes the documented tee-bypass (#63787). P1: the reentrancy guard the board specifies (E8-21.3 seq 236, E7-REQ11.1 seq 189) keys off a run_state DB column the loop driver writes, but the runtime delivers stop_hook_active on the Stop-event STDIN — and stop_hook.py main() never reads the top-level stop_hook_active field — so the documented ~8-9-block force-override guard (#63674) will not fire as designed. P1: all three on-disk hooks print the steering reason as JSON to STDOUT then exit 2, but on exit 2 the runtime ignores stdout/JSON and reads only STDERR (empty here) — the carefully written reason is dropped on every block — and NO board item owns this output-channel fix; the only references (E8-13.4 seq 615 "exit 2 blocks with stderr fed back", E4-S1.6 seq 259 "Assert stderr names…") test a contract the code violates. P2: subagent_stop_hook.py still emits decision:"approve", which is not a value in the current SubagentStop contract (allow = omit decision / exit 0); the board flags the rename only as a buried COH-3 note on E8-21 (seq 234), with no item owning the actual code edit + lockstep test. P2: best-practices doc 03 is now stale vs current docs in two places (additionalContext event list; missing PostToolBatch / the no-matcher event set). Bias of the prior pass: it correctly DECIDED the 5/6 scope and the Bash matcher in the artifact, but never propagated those decisions back onto the board ACs the executor will actually read — same root cause as GT-D3 (claimed UPDATEs that did not land their intended content).

findings.title: E8-13 settings-registration AC still says 6 hooks + PreCompact + matcher 'Write|Edit|MultiEdit' — contradicts the staged 01-settings.json (5 hooks, no PreCompact, matcher '...|Bash') the SAME pass authored
findings.evidence: Live snapshot + board-digest seq 211: task NAME = 'Author .claude/settings.json six-hook command registration'; DESC = 'Register Stop, PreToolUse, PostToolUse (matcher Write|Edit|MultiEdit), SubagentStop, SessionStart, PreCompact as type:command'. But the staged artifact audit/steering-audit/proposed/01-settings.json registers only 5 events (SessionStart, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SubagentStop, Stop), explicitly sets env SPINE_REQUIRED_EVENTS to those 5, and its _comment_governance says 'registers 5 of the 6 designed governance events ... PreCompact is deferred to D7 ... wired as-scoped at 5/6, not 6/6'. The prior report agrees (plane-reconciliation-report.md L280 'A1's five events are registered', L393 'five events; ralph disabled', and routes PreCompact to NEW-11/D7 at L155/L261). Separately, 01-settings.json PreToolUse matcher = 'Edit|Write|MultiEdit|Bash' (the Bash leg is the documented fix for the tee-bypass, anthropics/claude-code#63787, and best-practices doc G8), but the board AC matcher = 'Write|Edit|MultiEdit' — no Bash.
findings.claim_vs_reality: Board AC (the executor's contract) CLAIMS a 6-hook registration including PreCompact with an Edit/Write/MultiEdit matcher. REALITY: the staged settings.json + the prior report both committed to a 5-hook as-scoped spine (PreCompact deferred to NEW-11) with a 4-tool matcher that includes Bash. An executor building from seq 211 will (a) try to register a PreCompact command that does not exist on disk (.claude/hooks/ has no pre_compact hook per GT-3), producing a partial/broken spine, and (b) omit Bash from the PreToolUse matcher, re-opening the shell-redirection bypass the artifact closed. This is a direct sibling of GT-D3: a prior 'UPDATE' whose intended content never landed on the live AC.

recommended_action.detail: Rewrite seq 211 (story:ASCP-E8-13, task ASCP-E8-13.1) to match the as-scoped decision the prior pass already made in 01-settings.json: rename to '...five-hook (as-scoped) command registration'; DESC = 'Register Stop, PreToolUse (matcher Edit|Write|MultiEdit|Bash), PostToolUse (matcher Edit|Write|MultiEdit), SubagentStop, SessionStart (matcher startup|resume|compact) as type:command; set enabledPlugins ralph-loop@... false; set env SPINE_REQUIRED_EVENTS to the five registered events. PreCompact is the 6th designed event, DEFERRED to NEW-11 (D7) — do NOT register it here until its hook is authored.' Add the Bash matcher rationale (anti-tee-bypass, #63787). Cross-link NEW-11 (seq 262) as the item that re-adds PreCompact + re-widens SPINE_REQUIRED_EVENTS.

findings.title: Reentrancy guard reads stop_hook_active from the run_state DB row, but the runtime delivers it on Stop-event STDIN — stop_hook.py never reads it, so the documented anti-loop override will not fire as designed
findings.evidence: Board E8-21.3 (seq 236): 'with_reentrancy_guard(evaluate_stop) via run_state.stop_hook_active; release the flag only when the blocking condition clears'. E7-REQ11.1 (seq 189) and E8-14.3 (seq 217) add stop_hook_active as a run_state DB column. But current official docs (code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks; confirmed via web search of the Stop-hook input) state stop_hook_active is delivered on the hook's STDIN as a top-level boolean ('true if a previous Stop hook already kept Claude running'), and best-practices doc 03 §1.5/§1.8 + G2 say the Stop hook MUST read it from stdin and exit 0 when true. On disk, .claude/hooks/stop_hook.py main() reads only event.get('run_state') and event.get('feature_list') from stdin (lines 211-213) and never reads the top-level stop_hook_active; grep shows zero 'with_reentrancy_guard' or 'stop_hook_active' anywhere in .claude/hooks/ or tools/ — the guard is unimplemented. The run_state column is written by NEW-02's loop driver (seq 258), not by the Claude Code runtime.
findings.claim_vs_reality: Board CLAIMS the reentrancy guard is satisfied by a run_state.stop_hook_active column + a with_reentrancy_guard wrapper. REALITY: the runtime's own anti-loop signal arrives on stdin, not in run_state; keying the guard off a DB column the runtime never sets means a persistently-unproven item still blocks ~8-9× and is force-overridden ('A hook blocked the turn from ending 9 consecutive times' — anthropics/claude-code#63674, OPEN, reproduced on Opus 4.8 1M). The board's design and the official contract disagree on the source of truth for stop_hook_active.

recommended_action.detail: Amend E8-21.3 (seq 236) AC: 'The reentrancy guard MUST read the top-level stop_hook_active boolean from the Stop/SubagentStop event STDIN (the runtime-delivered flag) and exit 0/allow when true, per code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks. The run_state.stop_hook_active column (E7-REQ11.1) is a DURABLE MIRROR for the loop driver, NOT the gate input — the stdin flag is authoritative. Add a unit test feeding {stop_hook_active:true,...} on stdin and asserting allow()/exit 0 regardless of unproven items.' Add a relates_to edge E8-21 ↔ E7-REQ11 documenting the stdin-vs-DB split so they don't diverge.

findings.title: All three hooks print the steering reason as JSON to STDOUT then exit 2 — on exit 2 the runtime reads only STDERR (empty), so every block silently drops its reason; NO board item owns the output-channel fix
findings.evidence: On disk: pre_tool_use_hook.py main() does print(json.dumps(decision)) then 'return 0 if allow else 2' (lines 75-76); stop_hook.py main() identical (lines 214-215); subagent_stop_hook.py prints json then returns 2 (lines 109-110). Current docs are explicit: exit 2 = 'Ignores stdout/JSON. stderr fed back to Claude'; stdout JSON is parsed ONLY on exit 0 (code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks, Exit Code Semantics). best-practices doc 03 §1.1 + G4 already identified this ('on a block, Claude receives no steering text at all'). On the board, the only references are E8-13.4 (seq 615) Property 21 PBT 'exit 2 blocks with stderr fed back' and E4-S1.6 (seq 259) 'Assert stderr names…' — both ASSERT stderr behavior the implementation does not produce. No item carries an AC to actually route the reason to stderr (or switch to conforming exit-0 JSON with hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision for PreToolUse).
findings.claim_vs_reality: Board CLAIMS (via the Property 21 / E4-S1.6 ACs) that blocks feed steering text back on stderr. REALITY: the hooks write to stdout and the runtime discards it on exit 2, so the located, actionable 'reason' strings are never seen by Claude — the steering channel is dead on every block. The property test as written either fails against the current code or was authored to pass against the wrong (stdout) behavior. There is no owning item to fix the channel, only items that test the contract the code breaks.

recommended_action.detail: Create a NEW item (e.g. NEW-15, Module E8, Phase 0, type:wiring, gate:stop) 'Fix hook output channel: emit steering reason on the runtime-honored channel'. AC: on a block, write the reason to STDERR and exit 2 (PreToolUse/Stop/SubagentStop), OR switch to exit-0 conforming JSON (PreToolUse: hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision=deny + permissionDecisionReason + hookEventName; Stop/SubagentStop: top-level decision:block + reason). Stop printing the decision JSON to stdout on the exit-2 path. Make E8-13.4 (seq 615) Property 21 and E4-S1.6 (seq 259) blocked_by this item so the stderr assertions test real behavior. Cite code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks (exit-code semantics) and best-practices G4.

findings.title: subagent_stop_hook.py emits decision:'approve' — not a valid SubagentStop value in current docs (allow = omit decision/exit 0); board flags the rename only as a buried note, no item owns the code edit
findings.evidence: On disk subagent_stop_hook.py evaluate() returns {'decision':'approve',...} on the happy path (line 84) and main() maps 'approve'→exit 0 (line 110). Current docs: SubagentStop uses the top-level decision pattern whose 'Only value is "block". To allow, omit decision or exit 0' (code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks). best-practices doc 03 G3/G5 calls this out ('SubagentStop has no approve value'). The board mentions it only inside the E8-21 description (seq 234): 'COH-3 accept-value note (A4 approve->allow; update lockstep test)'. No standalone task or AC under E4-S3.4 (seq 134, the SubagentStop evidence/role gate) or E8-29.2 (seq 247, the omission_guard exit-2) owns changing 'approve'→omit and updating the lockstep test.
findings.claim_vs_reality: Board treats the approve→allow rename as an incidental note attached to E8-21 (whose real scope is HANDOFF ordering). REALITY: it is a concrete code change to subagent_stop_hook.py + its tests, and E4-S3.4 and E8-29.2 both build ON this hook assuming a correct accept value. As-is the non-conforming 'approve' is harmless only because exit 0 happens to allow — but the JSON is decorative/misleading and any future reader-of-JSON (e.g. additionalContext continuation) will misbehave.

recommended_action.detail: Add an explicit AC to E4-S3.4 (seq 134): 'subagent_stop_hook returns NO decision field (or omits it) on accept and exits 0; reserve decision:"block" + reason for objections; prefer hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext to continue the loop without the pre-2.1.163 error label. Update the lockstep approve→allow test.' Remove the rename from being only a buried note on E8-21 (seq 234) and relation: E8-29 (seq 247) blocked_by E4-S3.4.

findings.title: best-practices doc 03 is now stale vs current official docs: additionalContext event list and the no-matcher / PostToolBatch event set
findings.evidence: 03-best-practices.md §1.3 lists additionalContext as available on 'PreToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, SessionStart, and Stop/SubagentStop'. Current docs (code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks) list a wider set: SessionStart, Setup, UserPromptSubmit/UserPromptExpansion, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PostToolUseFailure, PostToolBatch, Stop, SubagentStop, SubagentStart. §1.7 of the doc lists the no-matcher events as 'UserPromptSubmit, Stop, PostToolBatch, CwdChanged, MessageDisplay' but the current docs add TeammateIdle, TaskCreated, TaskCompleted, WorktreeCreate, WorktreeRemove to the always-fire set, and PostToolUse now supports additionalContext + updatedToolOutput (the doc only notes updatedToolOutput). These are additive doc-currency gaps, not board defects, but the audit's §03 is cited by NEW-13 (seq 260) as a formal tier-1 standard.
findings.claim_vs_reality: The audit's best-practices reference CLAIMS to be 'authoritative ... from official docs' but predates current event coverage (PostToolUse additionalContext; expanded no-matcher set; PostToolBatch). REALITY: it is a point-in-time snapshot. No board item is wrong because of this, but NEW-13 formalizes the doc as a standard, so the staleness should be flagged before it is canonized.

recommended_action.detail: Flag for awareness on NEW-13 (seq 260): before canonizing 00-charter/§03 as the tier-1 standard, refresh §1.3 additionalContext event list and §1.7 no-matcher set against the current hooks reference (PostToolUse now supports additionalContext; add PostToolBatch/SubagentStart/Setup and the TeammateIdle/TaskCreated/TaskCompleted/WorktreeCreate no-matcher events). Re-pin the canonical-docs-fetched date. No live board item requires editing for this.

finders.summary: Live-API verification of the prior reconciliation's sequencing/dependency claims. THE central claim — that NEW-14 established blocked-by dependency edges from every hook-consuming story to E8-13 (settings.json) and the ralph-disable so the apply order cannot schedule a consumer ahead of the spine — is FALSE on the live board. I queried /work-items/{id}/relations/ for E8-13, NEW-14, and all 24 named hook-consuming stories (27 items total, 0.4s rate-limit): ALL 27 returned every one of the 8 relation buckets (blocking, blocked_by, duplicate, relates_to, start_after/before, finish_after/before) EMPTY. Not a single dependency edge exists anywhere on the board. This confirms and scales GT-D2 (E8-13 empty) to the entire consumer set. Root cause is structural: plane-upsert-plan.json contains ZERO relation ops (the string blocked_by appears 0 times; NEW-14's op fields are only [description_html, state, labels]) and the applier (.apply_log.json) never contains the substring relation — apply_upsert_plan.py has no relation-creation capability (GT-D2). So NEW-14 exists as a *story describing* edges that were never created. I also found a GT-D3-class collision: the UPD-E7-STATUS op named SEVEN true-up targets (REQ16/REQ6/REQ27/REQ11/REQ23/REQ12 + E6-S5) in one op, but the applier resolved it to ONE live id (ASCP-E7-REQ16, now Backlog) and silently dropped the other six — REQ6 still In-Verification, REQ27 Human-Review, REQ11 Agent-Executing, REQ23 Blocked, REQ12 Agent-Executing, E6-S5 Done — all states their absent on-disk tools/migrations cannot support (GT-3/5/6). Finally, the §4.4 phase/cycle reconciliations the report claims are split: S1/S2/S5 landed; S3 (E6-S6), S4 (E8-19), S6 (E6-S5), S7 (E7-REQ11/REQ12), S8 (E7-REQ23) did NOT — the applier never touched cycle assignment, leaving label!=cycle mismatches live. I provide the exact 25-endpoint blocked-by edge list (both verified live ids) as relation recommended_actions for separate application.

findings.title: NEW-14 dependency edges never landed: ALL 27 sampled items have empty blocked_by — the spine-first topology the report guarantees does not exist
findings.evidence: Live GET /work-items/{id}/relations/ for E8-13 (story:ASCP-E8-13, id 110f71c8-d196-482d-a807-fc6463874523, seq 55), NEW-14 (ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-14, seq 251) and all 24 hook-consuming stories in NEW-14 AC1 returned every bucket empty: blocked_by:[] blocking:[] relates_to:[] duplicate:[] start_after:[] start_before:[] finish_after:[] finish_before:[] — 27/27 items, no exceptions. Confirms+scales GT-D2. plane-upsert-plan.json has 0 relation operations (substring blocked_by appears 0x; operations[3]=NEW-14 has fields [description_html,state,labels] only). .apply_log.json never contains the substring 'relation' (apply_upsert_plan.py cannot create relations, GT-D2).
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: reconciliation-report.md §5.1 NEW-14 AC1/AC2 + §6 Active-Sprint ('NEW-14 ensures the sprint dependency-ordering cannot schedule a hook-consuming story ahead of E8-13') + §7 Milestone-1.3 ('add the explicit blocked-by relations ... so the rest of the apply order cannot violate the spine-first topology') assert these edges as applied board topology. REALITY: zero edges exist; the apply tool has no relation capability and the plan never encoded any. NEW-14 is a description-only placeholder. An executor reading the board sees no dependency guard and can freely schedule a hook-consuming story (e.g. E5-S10, E8-21) before E8-13 registers the hooks they assume fire.

recommended_action.detail: Create 25 blocked_by relations via POST /work-items/{consumer_id}/relations/ with E8-13 (id 110f71c8-d196-482d-a807-fc6463874523, story:ASCP-E8-13) as the blocker. Endpoints (consumer --blocked_by--> E8-13): story:E2-S1, story:E2-S2, story:E2-S3, story:ASCP-E3-1, story:ASCP-E3-2, story:ASCP-E3-4, story:ASCP-E3-22, story:ASCP-E4-S1, story:ASCP-E4-S2, story:ASCP-E4-S3, story:ASCP-E4-S4, story:ASCP-E5-S10, story:E6-S2, story:E6-S3, story:E6-S7, story:ASCP-E7-REQ6, story:ASCP-E7-REQ11, story:ASCP-E7-REQ12, story:ASCP-E7-REQ16, story:ASCP-E7-REQ23, story:ASCP-E7-REQ27, story:ASCP-E8-14, story:ASCP-E8-18, story:ASCP-E8-21, story:ASCP-E8-26. (AC2 ralph-disable leg also targets E8-13 since the report folds the ralph-disable into E8-13 AC2/AC5, so the same 25 edges satisfy both AC1 and AC2.) All 25 consumer external_ids verified present on the board with empty buckets. Then re-PATCH NEW-14 (seq 251) state Spec-Verified->Done only after the relations read back.

findings.title: UPD-E7-STATUS batch op silently true-upped only 1 of its 7 named targets (apply-collision class, GT-D3 sibling)
findings.evidence: .apply_log.json action 'E7 + E6-S5 status true-up (REQ16/REQ6/REQ27/REQ11/REQ23/REQ12 + E6-S5)' has id=73e5a0ce-a0df-4063-a539-b08bce19396e, which resolves in the snapshot to a SINGLE item: seq 54 = ASCP-E7-REQ16 (now state=Backlog, and its desc carries the C16 true-up text 'E7 stories carry advanced states ... status claims outrun ...'). The plan op UPD-E7-STATUS declared state=Backlog for all 7. Live states of the other 6: ASCP-E7-REQ6=In-Verification, ASCP-E7-REQ27=Human-Review, ASCP-E7-REQ11=Agent-Executing, ASCP-E7-REQ23=Blocked, ASCP-E7-REQ12=Agent-Executing, E6-S5=Done. REQ11 and E6-S5 still show their ORIGINAL EARS descriptions (no true-up note), proving they were never PATCHed.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report §4.1 C16 + §6 Workflow-Kanban ('READY after apply ... E7 stories off Done/In-Verification (C16)') asserts all six E7 stories + E6-S5 were trued-up off advanced states. REALITY: 6 of 7 remain in advanced states (Done/In-Verification/Human-Review/Agent-Executing/Blocked) while their underlying tools/migrations/telemetry are absent on disk (GT-3 no session_start/post_tool_use; GT-5 zero .sql; GT-6 no spec_validator/execution_bounds). The applier's 1-op->1-name->1-id resolution collapsed a 7-target batch into one PATCH. Kanban view is NOT execution-ready as claimed.

recommended_action.detail: Re-issue six separate state PATCHes to Backlog (or the report's intended true-up state): ASCP-E7-REQ6, ASCP-E7-REQ27, ASCP-E7-REQ11, ASCP-E7-REQ23, ASCP-E7-REQ12, and E6-S5 (seq 46). Going forward, forbid multi-target batch ops in the upsert plan: each true-up target must be its own op with a unique external_id match_key so name->id resolution cannot drop siblings.

findings.title: S3/S4/S6/S7/S8 phase-or-state reconciliations claimed in §4.4 never landed (applier never wrote cycle assignment)
findings.evidence: Every UPD action in .apply_log.json carries fields=[description_html,state,labels] — cycle is never in the field set, so no op moved an item between cycles. Live: S3 ASCP-E6-S6 (seq 47) label phase:3 but cycle 'Phase 2', state In-Verification — neither re-phased to its phase:0 consumer as §4.4-S3 said. S4 ASCP-E8-19 (seq 59) label phase:3, cycle 'Phase 5', AC says Phase 6 — triple mismatch, §4.4-S4 unfixed. S6 E6-S5 (seq 46) still Done with no evidence, label phase:3 vs cycle Phase 2 — §4.4-S6 unfixed. S7 ASCP-E7-REQ11 (seq 50) carries THREE phase labels (phase:0,phase:1,phase:2) in cycle 'Phase 3', state Agent-Executing; ASCP-E7-REQ12 (seq 51) phase:3 label, Agent-Executing — §4.4-S7 unfixed. S8 ASCP-E7-REQ23 (seq 53) Blocked with empty relation buckets (verified live) — §4.4-S8 'encode the blocker relation or move off Blocked' unfixed. (For contrast S1 E5-S31 phase:4+Phase4 and S2 E6-S3 phase:0+Phase0 and S5 E3-22 phase:0+Phase0 DID land coherently.)
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report §6 'By Phase READY after apply' and 'Workflow Kanban READY after apply' enumerate S1-S8 as reconciled. REALITY: only S1/S2/S5 are coherent; S3/S4/S6/S7/S8 still have label!=cycle, an AC-contradicting phase, a Done-without-evidence, a multi-phase-labeled story, and a Blocked-without-link. The §4.4 note 'S3/S4/S6 are field/relation edits the apply step performs' was never executed because the applier cannot set cycles and was never asked to.

recommended_action.detail: Apply the missing field edits directly: E6-S6 -> single phase label matching its phase:0 consumer + move to Phase 0 cycle (or whatever its real consumer phase is) and correct agent_role; E8-19 -> phase:6 label + Phase 6 cycle (per its AC); E6-S5 -> move off Done until evidence exists + reconcile phase:3-label/Phase-2-cycle; E7-REQ11 -> collapse the three phase labels to one and align label/cycle, true-up state; E7-REQ12 -> align; E7-REQ23 -> either add the blocked_by edge (its real prereq is NEW-11 D7 + migrations NEW-12) or move off Blocked. These require setting the cycle field, which the current applier does not support — handle out-of-band or extend the tool.

findings.title: Phase-ordering hazard: D7 PreCompact producer (NEW-11) is phase:2 while a phase:2-labeled consumer (E7-REQ23) sits in a Phase-3 cycle, with no edge to enforce producer-before-consumer
findings.evidence: NEW-11 (D7 PreCompact resume_state_hash producer, ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-11, seq 262) = phase:2 / cycle 'Phase 2', state Backlog. Its consumer E7-REQ23 (resume-integrity, seq 53) = phase:2 label but cycle 'Phase 3', state Blocked, relation buckets empty (verified). E7-REQ11 task #2 (ASCP-E7-REQ11.3, board seq under story 50) IS itself 'pre_compact_hook.py checkpoint writer + baseline-hash producer' — overlapping ownership with NEW-11, but REQ11 is labeled phase:0/1/2 in a Phase-3 cycle. No blocked_by edge ties REQ23 (consumer) to NEW-11 or REQ11.3 (producer).
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report §3.3/§5 treats D7 as cleanly owned by NEW-11 with A-AMBIG-01 integrity-half gated on it. REALITY: the PreCompact producer is split across NEW-11 (phase:2) and ASCP-E7-REQ11.3 (a phase:0/1/2-labeled task) with no cross-link, and the resume-integrity consumer E7-REQ23 has no edge to either producer and a label/cycle mismatch. An executor cannot tell from the board that REQ23 is blocked on NEW-11/REQ11.3, nor that NEW-11 and REQ11.3 are the same artifact. This is a sequencing ambiguity, not a hard phase:0-on-phase:2 inversion (the phase:0 D3 consumers E5-S10/E8-21/E8-14/E3-4 are correctly co-phased with their phase:0 producer NEW-02).

recommended_action.detail: Add blocked_by edges: ASCP-E7-REQ23 --blocked_by--> NEW-11 (ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-11) and ASCP-E7-REQ23 --blocked_by--> NEW-12 (the migrations, once NEW-12 is created per GT-D1). Add a relates_to edge ASCP-E7-REQ11 <-> NEW-11 to make the duplicate PreCompact-checkpoint ownership explicit, or fold REQ11.3 into NEW-11. Separately reconcile E7-REQ23 and E7-REQ11 phase label vs cycle (see PSD-03).

finders.dimension: APPLY-INTEGRITY — did the prior reconciliation apply actually land what it claimed on the live board?
finders.summary: The prior apply did NOT land as the report/plan claim. Diffing the apply_log + upsert-plan against the live board (256 items, verified via snapshot AND live API) exposes five distinct apply-layer defect classes, all caused by `plane-integration/apply_upsert_plan.py` being a description/state/labels-only PATCHer that cannot create relations, cannot set priority, and resolves ops by a single match_key. CONFIRMED COUNTS: the report claims new:14/update:25 (plan _meta.counts), but the applier's own log records 13 creates + 26 updates, and the live board carries exactly 13 audit roots (NEW-01..11,13,14). NEW-12 was never created — it was logged as the FIRST of two `update` ops onto df8b5217 (REQ-INFRA-005) and the second op clobbered it (GT-D1, mechanism now proven from the log itself). FIVE classes found: (1) GT-D1 NEW-12 lost to collision; (2) GT-D2 NEW-14's ~24 blocked-by edges never created — live relations endpoint shows ALL buckets empty on E8-13/E4-S1/NEW-14/REQ23; (3) NEW-09 was created as a live Backlog item despite being marked supersedes/conditional-skip once UPD-E8-20 applied (which it did) — now a duplicate execution_bounds owner; (4) UPD-E7-STATUS intended to true-up 7 items via match_key+6 additional_match_keys, but the applier ignores additional_match_keys — only REQ16 was patched; REQ6/27/11/23/12 + E6-S5 are UNCHANGED (still In-Verification/Human-Review/Agent-Executing/Blocked/Done), so C16/S6/S7/S8 are all UNRESOLVED on the live board despite the report claiming the Workflow-Kanban view is READY; (5) the `priority:*` label was never applied to ANY of the 13 created items (applier never reads op['priority']) — the Priority-Triage view the report calls READY is empty. The .apply_log.json carries no per-op result/status/error, so it is a plan-echo, not an outcome log — which is why none of these silent drops were caught. Additionally the §4.4 'recommended' field-edits S3 (E6-S6) and S4 (E8-19) were never encoded as ops and never landed.

findings.title: NEW-12 (eight .kiro migrations) was never created — it was logged as the first of two updates onto REQ-INFRA-005 and clobbered; the report's new:14 count is false (live = 13)
findings.evidence: .apply_log.json contains TWO update actions on id df8b5217-d915-4194-a640-2ba8b0b7f827: action 1 name='Author the eight .kiro migrations 001-008…' (this IS NEW-12), action 2 name='REQ-INFRA-005 — migration set provisioning (true-up AC#4)'. apply_upsert_plan.py resolve() (lines 138-148) maps both ops to the same live id via match_key 'story:REQ-INFRA-005', so NEW-12 took the update branch (line 195-202), never the create branch (203-217). Live API GET /work-items/df8b5217/ description_html = '…Reframe AC#4 to depend on NEW-12 authoring the migrations…' — the SECOND op's text; NEW-12's AC1-AC4 (author 8 migration files, run_state completeness, gate_audit_log append-only grant) is gone. create-log lists exactly NEW-01..11,13,14 — NEW-12 absent. Snapshot confirms 13 ascp-audit-reorg roots, not 14.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report §0/§1/§5 + plan _meta.counts.new=14 say 14 NEW owning items were created, including NEW-12 as E1's migration-authoring owner (report §2 E1 row, §5 table, milestone-1 step 2). REALITY: only 13 created; NEW-12 has no owning item; REQ-INFRA-005 now dangles a reference to a non-existent NEW-12. E1's load-bearing migration/run_state/gate_audit_log authoring has no home on the board.

recommended_action.detail: Re-create NEW-12 as a standalone item external_id ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-12 (E1 module, Phase 0 cycle, labels phase:0/type:wiring/gate:security, state Spec-Verified) carrying the plan's NEW-12 description+AC1-4 (author requirements/coverage_items/traceability_links/evidence_records/run_state/domain_baseline_checklists/requirement_versions/gate_audit_log migrations; run_state all D3/D7 columns; gate_audit_log append-only grant). Must use the live /relations/ or a distinct external_id so it does NOT resolve back onto REQ-INFRA-005 — the upsert match_key 'story:REQ-INFRA-005' is what caused the collision, so NEW-12 must be created with match_key=null and a guard that the create branch is taken.

findings.title: Applier has no relation capability — NEW-14's ~24 blocked-by edges to E8-13 + ralph-disable were never created (GT-D2); the spine-first topology the report calls enforced does not exist
findings.evidence: Live GET /work-items/{id}/relations/ returns all-zero buckets {blocking:0, blocked_by:0, relates_to:0, …} for NEW-14, E8-13, and E4-S1 (a representative hook-consumer). apply_upsert_plan.py main loop (lines 184-217) only PATCHes description_html/state/labels + POSTs module-issues/cycle-issues (assign(), lines 170-180); there is NO POST to /work-items/{id}/relations/ anywhere in the file. NEW-14's own AC1 lists ~24 hook-consuming stories that must carry a blocked-by edge to E8-13, and AC2 the ralph-disable edge — none exist.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report §2 cross-epic note + §5 NEW-14 + §6 Active-Sprint say NEW-14 'turns that assumption into enforced board topology (explicit blocked-by edges) so apply order cannot schedule a hook-consuming story ahead of E8-13'. REALITY: NEW-14 exists only as a story describing edges; zero relations were created; the applier is structurally incapable of creating them. The dependency-ordering guarantee the Active-Sprint/Roadmap views rely on is absent.

recommended_action.detail: Add blocked_by relations via POST /work-items/{consumer}/relations/ for each AC1 endpoint → E8-13 (110f71c8): E2-S1/S2/S3, ASCP-E3-1/-2/-4/-22, ASCP-E4-S1/S2/S3/S4, ASCP-E5-S10, E6-S2/S3/S7, ASCP-E7-REQ6/REQ11/REQ12/REQ16/REQ23/REQ27, ASCP-E8-14/18/21/26. Extend apply_upsert_plan.py with a relations-POST path (it currently lacks one) before re-running, or apply the edges with a dedicated relation script. Minimum: NEW-14 itself should at least relates_to E8-13 so it is not a content-only orphan.

findings.title: UPD-E7-STATUS silently patched only 1 of 7 intended targets — applier ignores additional_match_keys; C16/S6/S7/S8 are all UNRESOLVED on the live board
findings.evidence: Plan op UPD-E7-STATUS has match_key 'story:ASCP-E7-REQ16' + additional_match_keys [REQ6,REQ27,REQ11,REQ23,REQ12,E6-S5]. apply_upsert_plan.py resolve() (lines 138-148) reads only op['match_key'] (and a single ASCP regex token / by_name) — it never reads additional_match_keys. apply_log shows exactly ONE update on id 73e5a0ce (REQ16). Live board: REQ16 = Backlog with the C16 true-up text (landed). The other six are UNTOUCHED: REQ6=In-Verification, REQ27=Human-Review, REQ11=Agent-Executing+label human:review, REQ12=Agent-Executing+human:review, REQ23=Blocked (live /relations/ all-zero — no blocker edge, S8), E6-S5=Done (no in-slice evidence, S6). Their desc_text is still raw EARS, not the C16 fix.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report §6 Workflow-Kanban 'READY after apply — E7 stories off Done/In-Verification (C16); E6-S5 off Done (S6); E7-REQ23 Blocked-without-link reconciled (S8); E7 agent_role:human+Agent-Executing mismatch fixed (S7)'. REALITY: only REQ16 changed. Five E7 stories remain in evidence-outrunning states, REQ11/REQ12 still pair human:review with Agent-Executing (S7 unresolved), REQ23 is still a dead-end Blocked with no edge (S8 unresolved), E6-S5 is still Done without evidence (S6 unresolved). The Kanban-view 'no item in a state its evidence cannot support' guarantee is false.

recommended_action.detail: Apply the C16 true-up individually to each of REQ6/REQ27/REQ11/REQ23/REQ12 and E6-S5: state→Backlog (or Spec-Verified once prereqs land), strip human:review where it co-occurs with Agent-Executing (S7), and for REQ23 either POST a blocked_by relation to its real blocker (NEW-11/NEW-12) or move it off Blocked (S8); move E6-S5 off Done (S6). Also fix apply_upsert_plan.py resolve() to honor additional_match_keys so a single op can fan out, or split UPD-E7-STATUS into 7 single-target ops.

findings.title: priority:* label was never set on any of the 13 created items — applier drops op['priority']; the Priority-Triage view the report calls READY is empty for the spine items
findings.evidence: Plan ops carry priority as a TOP-LEVEL field, e.g. NEW-01/02/05/08 + UPD-E8-13 all have 'priority':'priority:blocking', separate from the labels[] array. apply_upsert_plan.py body-builder (lines 186-193) reads only description_html, state, and labels — there is no read of op['priority'] and no mapping into the native priority field. Live board: NEW-01 labels=[type:wiring,gate:stop,phase:0] (no priority:blocking), same for NEW-02/NEW-05; E8-13 labels=[human:review,type:wiring,gate:stop,phase:0] (no priority:blocking). Across all 256 items only 32 carry any priority:* label and all 32 are pre-existing ascp-kiro items; the 13 audit roots have native priority='none'.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report §6 Priority-Triage 'READY after apply — the 5 priority:blocking items (NEW-01/02/05/08/12 + E8-13) are exactly the spine-critical path'. REALITY: none of those items carry priority:blocking (and NEW-12 doesn't exist). Grouping by priority:* surfaces zero spine items; triage is non-functional for the reconciliation set.

recommended_action.detail: Add the priority:* label (and/or native priority) to each created item per the plan: priority:blocking on NEW-01/02/05/08 + E8-13 + NEW-12-when-recreated; priority:high on NEW-03/04/09/10/14; priority:normal on NEW-06/07/11/13. Patch apply_upsert_plan.py to fold op['priority'] into the label_ids set (or set the native priority field) so re-runs are idempotent on priority.

findings.title: NEW-09 was created as a live duplicate execution_bounds owner despite supersedes/conditional-skip — UPD-E8-20 (the preferred fold) also landed, so two items now own tools/execution_bounds
findings.evidence: Plan NEW-09 carries 'conditional':'only if UPD-E8-20 not applied' and UPD-E8-20 carries 'supersedes':'NEW-09'. UPD-E8-20 DID apply: live seq 60 desc_text = 'Expand E8-20 to OWN authoring tools/execution_bounds (PREFERRED over a standalone item)…'. Yet NEW-09 was also created (apply_log create-list includes ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-09; live seq 256 exists in Backlog). apply_upsert_plan.py has no conditional/supersedes handling — it executes every op in the array unconditionally (loop line 184), so the guard was never honored.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: plan/report say NEW-09 is the fallback owner, created only if the E8-20 fold is rejected; report §3.3 '(prefer tightening E8-20)'. REALITY: both UPD-E8-20 and NEW-09 landed, creating two competing owners of tools/execution_bounds — the exact duplicate-owner ambiguity the reconciliation was meant to remove. NEW-09's description even says 'executor should prefer the E8-20 fold and skip this create'.

recommended_action.detail: Resolve the duplication on ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-09: either cancel/close it (it is in Backlog so low-blast-radius) with a note pointing to ASCP-E8-20 as the live owner, or relates_to E8-20 and re-label it as a superseded placeholder. Do NOT leave two Spec-Verified/Backlog items both claiming to author tools/execution_bounds. Long-term, teach the applier to honor 'conditional'/'supersedes' so such guard ops are skipped.

findings.title: .apply_log.json is a plan-echo with no per-op outcome — no result/HTTP-status/error field, which is exactly why the silent drops (NEW-12 collision, dropped relations, dropped priority, 6 untouched E7 stories) went uncaught
findings.evidence: apply_upsert_plan.py builds each log entry as {'op','name','id'|'external_id','fields'} only (lines 202 and 217) — fields is list(body), i.e. which keys were SENT, not whether the API accepted them or what id resulted. No status code, no response body, no error capture. The log records 39 actions (13 create + 26 update) and the second df8b5217 update sits silently next to the first with no 'clobber' or 'collision' signal. Because the log mirrors the plan rather than the API outcome, a reader cannot tell NEW-12 was lost, that additional_match_keys were ignored, or that priority/relations were never sent.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: the existence of .apply_log.json with 39 actions implies a faithful applied record matching plan _meta.counts (new:14/update:25). REALITY: the log says new:13/update:26 (NEW-12 demoted to an update), carries no success confirmation, and cannot surface any of the five defects above — it is a write-intent echo, so 'it's in the apply log' is not evidence the effect landed.

recommended_action.detail: Flag for awareness + tooling fix: extend apply_upsert_plan.py to capture per-op {http_status, returned_id, error, collided_with} and to assert post-apply that each create yielded a NEW external_id (not a resolve-to-existing). Add a reconciliation step that re-GETs each touched item and diffs sent-vs-landed description_html/state/labels. Until then, the live board (not the log) is the only valid apply-integrity oracle.

findings.title: §4.4 'recommended' field-edits S3 (E6-S6) and S4 (E8-19) were never encoded as ops and never landed — phase label vs cycle vs AC incoherences persist
findings.evidence: Report §4.4 marks S3 (E6-S6: re-phase span-contract from phase:3 to its phase:0 consumer + correct agent_role) and S4 (E8-19: reconcile phase:3 label to its Phase-6 AC) as 'recommended'/'executor applies the field edit', but neither appears as an op in plane-upsert-plan.json operations[] and neither id is in apply_log. Live: E6-S6 = label phase:3, cycle 'Phase 2 — Durable State & Security', state In-Verification (unreconciled, three-way mismatch). E8-19 = label phase:3, cycle 'Phase 5 — Durable Orchestration (optional)', state Agent-Executing, AC says Phase 6 (still incoherent). By contrast the explicitly-opped S1/C9 (E5-S31) DID land cleanly (phase:4 label + Phase 4 cycle).
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report §6 By-Phase view 'READY after apply — E6-S6 (S3), E8-19 (S4) label↔cycle/AC reconciled'. REALITY: S3 and S4 were never turned into executable ops, so both items retain their label/cycle/AC contradictions; the By-Phase grouping is still incoherent for these two.

recommended_action.detail: Encode the two missing edits as real ops: E6-S6 → set label/cycle to phase:0 to match its consumer (per S3) and correct agent_role from 'research' to the implementer role per the COH-1 <role>.md map; E8-19 → set label phase:6 and move to the Phase-6 cycle to match its own AC (per S4). These are simple field/label edits, not new stories.

finders.summary: The prior pass's single most-load-bearing state claim — report line 367, "Workflow Kanban READY after apply: E7 off Done/In-Verification (C16), E6-S5 off Done (S6), REQ23 Blocked-without-link reconciled (S8), agent_role:human+Agent-Executing fixed (S7); no item sits in a state its evidence cannot support" — is FALSE on the live board. The C16/S6/S7/S8 true-up was authored as ONE op (UPD-E7-STATUS, upsert-plan operations[38]) with match_key=story:ASCP-E7-REQ16 plus SIX additional_match_keys (REQ6, REQ27, REQ11, REQ23, REQ12, E6-S5), all targeted to state=Backlog. The applier (per GT-D1/D3, the same collision class) honored only the primary match_key: .apply_log.json records "E7 + E6-S5 status true-up" as a single update action, and live API confirms ONLY REQ16 moved (updated_at 2026-06-18T17:40, state=Backlog, C16 desc present). The other six are untouched at the 2026-06-17T22:32 creation timestamp, retaining BOTH their original advanced states AND their original EARS descriptions (C16 true-up text absent from all six). Live states: REQ6=In-Verification, REQ27=Human-Review, REQ11=Agent-Executing, REQ23=Blocked, REQ12=Agent-Executing, E6-S5=Done — every one a state its on-disk evidence cannot support (GT-1..6: spine unwired, no tools, no SQL). S8 independently confirmed live: REQ23 /work-items/{id}/relations/ returns all 8 buckets EMPTY (blocked_by:0) — a dead-end Blocked, sibling of GT-D2 (applier has no relation-creation capability). S7 mismatch is live-visible: REQ11/REQ12 carry human:review label while in Agent-Executing. Separately, the C16 op SCOPE itself was too narrow: E6-S6, E8-29, E8-26 (In-Verification) and E8-19, E8-15 (Agent-Executing) are equally evidence-impossible state-incoherences that were never enumerated in the op at all. Net: the C16/S6/S7/S8 milestone is ~14% applied (1 of 7 targets), and the Workflow-Kanban-readiness claim is the prior pass's largest single overstatement.

findings.title: C16/S6/S7/S8 state true-up landed on only 1 of 7 targets — six additional_match_keys silently dropped (sibling of GT-D1/D3)
findings.evidence: plane-upsert-plan.json operations[38] (UPD-E7-STATUS): match_key='story:ASCP-E7-REQ16', additional_match_keys=[REQ6,REQ27,REQ11,REQ23,REQ12,E6-S5], state='Backlog'. .apply_log.json records it as ONE update action ('E7 + E6-S5 status true-up …', fields=[description_html,state,labels]). LIVE API (read-only, 2026-06-18): REQ16 state=Backlog updated_at=2026-06-18T17:40:56 (moved + C16 desc present); REQ6=In-Verification, REQ27=Human-Review, REQ11=Agent-Executing, REQ23=Blocked, REQ12=Agent-Executing, E6-S5=Done — all six updated_at=2026-06-17T22:32 (untouched creation stamp). Snapshot desc_text: the C16 true-up text ('status claims outrun on-disk reality') is present ONLY on REQ16; the other six retain their original EARS descriptions. GT-1..6 establish nothing is built, so In-Verification/Agent-Executing/Done are unsupportable.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report line 187 + upsert-plan rationale assert C16 trues-up 'each E7 story (REQ16/6/27/11/23/12) + E6-S5' to Backlog/Spec-Verified, and .apply_log shows the op ran. REALITY: the applier resolves a single op to a single live id (its name->external_id resolution ignores additional_match_keys), so 6 of 7 targets kept both their advanced state and their pre-true-up description. The milestone is ~14% applied. This is the exact GT-D1/D3 collision class, not a one-off.

recommended_action.detail: Issue six separate state PATCHes (state -> Backlog id d83bd05d-f8a2-4440-ad90-00596609af6c) to REQ6 (a997f0cd), REQ27 (61efbf2f), REQ11 (f7f107f9), REQ23 (09176986), REQ12 (51779832), E6-S5 (f07788c7), and re-apply the C16 true-up description_html to each. Root-cause fix: change apply_upsert_plan.py to fan out additional_match_keys into N independent PATCH calls (one resolved id each) and emit one apply_log action per resolved id, so multi-target ops can never collapse to one.

findings.title: S8 NOT landed: REQ23 still in Blocked with zero blocker relations (dead-end Blocked, sibling of GT-D2)
findings.evidence: LIVE GET /work-items/09176986-91b3-4618-a233-85e90c40c6d0/relations/ -> HTTP 200, all 8 buckets = 0 (blocking:0, blocked_by:0, relates_to:0, …). LIVE state = Blocked (cancelled group, id 8ea88a3c). upsert-plan AC3(S8): 'REQ23 either carries an encoded blocker relation OR is moved off Blocked (no dead-end Blocked with no edge).' Neither happened: state still Blocked, blocked_by still empty. REQ23's own desc names its prereq as the SessionStart/PreCompact resume-integrity chain (NEW-11 PreCompact, plus migrations).
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report line 238 + line 367 say S8 is 'reconciled' (REQ23 Blocked-without-link either gets a blocker edge or moves off Blocked) and the Kanban is honest after apply. REALITY: REQ23 sits in the cancelled-group Blocked terminal with no blocked_by edge — the Kanban shows a blocked item with no cause. The applier (GT-D2) cannot POST relations, so no edge was ever creatable by this tool, and the state PATCH that would have moved it off Blocked is the same one swallowed in WF-01.

recommended_action.detail: Preferred: move REQ23 off Blocked to Backlog (per WF-01 PATCH), since its true blocker is 'prereqs absent' not an in-board blocker. If Blocked is retained, add blocked_by edges from REQ23 (09176986) to its real prereqs — NEW-11 PreCompact resume_state_hash hook (262 ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-11) and the migration owner (see GT-D1: NEW-12 does not exist; use REQ-INFRA-005 df8b5217 until NEW-12 is created) — via POST /work-items/09176986/relations/ with relation_type=blocked_by. A Blocked state with no blocked_by edge must not persist.

findings.title: S7 NOT landed: REQ11 & REQ12 still Agent-Executing while carrying human:review (human-owned item cannot be agent-executing)
findings.evidence: LIVE state REQ11 (f7f107f9)=Agent-Executing, REQ12 (51779832)=Agent-Executing (both state id b7b5ff8a, started group), both untouched updated_at=2026-06-17T22:32. Snapshot labels: REQ11=[human:review,priority:high,type:functional,phase:0/1/2]; REQ12=[human:review,priority:normal,type:functional,gate:stop,phase:3]. The board-visible proxy for agent_role:human is the human:review label co-existing with an Agent-Executing (agent-driven) state. (Note: the v1 API does not expose issue-type custom-property values — /work-items/{id}/issue-properties/ etc. all 404 — so agent_role is verified via its label/desc proxy, not a custom-field read.)
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report line 237 + AC2(S7) say the agent_role:human + Agent-Executing mismatch on REQ11/REQ12/REQ23 is 'folded into UPD-E7-STATUS and reconciled,' Kanban honest after apply. REALITY: the contradictory pairing is fully live on REQ11 and REQ12 (human-review + Agent-Executing). The reconciliation depended on the same swallowed state PATCH (WF-01).

recommended_action.detail: Resolved by the WF-01 PATCH batch (REQ11/REQ12 -> Backlog). Add a guard note to the executor: any item with human:review (or agent_role=human) may not occupy an agent-driven started state (Agent-Executing/Spec-Compiling/In-Verification by an agent). Optionally add a board-integrity property test (extend NEW-14 / E6-S7's anti-Plane-gating guard) asserting human-owned items never sit in agent-execution states.

findings.title: S6 NOT landed: E6-S5 still Done with no in-slice evidence for a blocking reconciliation item
findings.evidence: LIVE state E6-S5 (f07788c7)=Done (completed group, id d30f24ad), updated_at=2026-06-17T22:32 (untouched). E6-S5 is 'Bidirectional state-mirror and traceability-triple sync' (priority:blocking, gate:completion) — depends on a live webhook board + Postgres traceability store, none of which exist (GT-5 zero *.sql; GT-1 spine unwired). Snapshot desc_text carries no C16/evidence note. It is the board's ONLY Done item (GT-12: Done=1).
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report line 236 + line 367 + AC4(S6) say E6-S5 is 'moved off Done until in-slice evidence exists.' REALITY: it is still the single Done item on the board, with zero supporting on-disk artifacts. A completed-group state on a blocking item with no evidence is the strongest single readiness misrepresentation in the Kanban (it implies the bidirectional sync is shipped).

recommended_action.detail: PATCH E6-S5 (f07788c7) state Done -> Backlog (d83bd05d) as part of the WF-01 batch. Done must be reachable only via the Stop+OPA+required-check path (its own AC, and C11 on E6-S3); until those gates exist and fire, no item may sit in the completed group.

findings.title: C16 op SCOPE was too narrow: 5 further evidence-impossible advanced-state stories were never enumerated for true-up
findings.evidence: LIVE/snapshot story-level advanced states beyond the 7 C16 targets: E6-S6=In-Verification (integration-layer OTel spans; telemetry absent), ASCP-E8-29=In-Verification (omission-declaration gate; subagent_stop hook/schema absent GT-3/GT-2), ASCP-E8-26=In-Verification (reasoning-loop detection; telemetry absent), ASCP-E8-19=Agent-Executing (predictive routing), ASCP-E8-15=Agent-Executing (inner-loop orchestration — but the spine is unwired, GT-1, so nothing can be agent-executing). The UPD-E7-STATUS op enumerates only REQ16/6/27/11/23/12 + E6-S5 (operations[38]); none of these five appear in any match_key. C1 separately handled E8-13 off Agent-Executing, but these five were missed by both C1 and C16.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report line 367 generalizes 'No item sits in a state its on-disk evidence cannot support' after apply. REALITY: even had the C16 op applied to all 7 of its targets, five more story-level items (E6-S6, E8-29, E8-26, E8-19, E8-15) would still be in In-Verification/Agent-Executing with zero supporting artifacts — the universal claim was never achievable from the op's scope. The prior pass mis-scoped the state-incoherence class to E7+E6-S5 only.

recommended_action.detail: Extend the true-up to these five: PATCH E6-S6, E8-29, E8-26 (In-Verification) and E8-19, E8-15 (Agent-Executing) to Backlog or Spec-Verified per the same C16 rule (Agent-Executing/In-Verification require a wired spine + the consumed tool to exist on disk; neither holds). E8-15 specifically must not be Agent-Executing while E8-13/NEW-01 (settings.json + spine canary) are themselves Spec-Verified-not-built.

findings.title: Prior report's 'Workflow Kanban READY after apply' verdict is unverified-and-false; downstream readers will trust an inert state surface
findings.evidence: plane-reconciliation-report.md line 367 marks the Workflow-Kanban view 'READY after apply' and enumerates five state true-ups (C1/C16/S6/S7/S8) as done. WF-01..WF-05 show 6 of 7 C16 targets unmoved, REQ23 Blocked-without-edge live, E6-S5 still Done, and 5 further items out of scope. The report records no post-apply read-back verification of the live states (the apply_log only echoes intended fields, not realized server state — exactly the GT-D3 hazard).
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: the report presents a green 'READY' Kanban verdict as a settled outcome. REALITY: the verdict was derived from the PLAN, not a live read-back; the live board contradicts it on every C16/S6/S7/S8 leg except REQ16. An executor consuming the Workflow-Kanban view to pick work will see false readiness (Done/Agent-Executing/In-Verification on unbuilt items).

recommended_action.detail: Flag for awareness: do not treat report line 367 as ground truth. After the WF-01..WF-05 PATCHes land, perform a live read-back (GET each /work-items/{id}/ + /relations/) and only then mark the Kanban honest. Add a standing rule to the applier: every UPDATE/field-edit op must be confirmed by a post-PATCH GET of the realized state before being logged as applied (closes the GT-D3 class report-wide).

finders.summary: The prior pass authored good, mostly-runnable acceptance criteria in plane-upsert-plan.json (NEW-01..14 each carry an acceptance_criteria[] array; e.g. NEW-01 AC1 "emits 'Spine wired: 5 governance event(s) registered'", NEW-05 AC3 "a CI assert ... byte-for-byte", NEW-06's four teardown predicates) — but the applier apply_upsert_plan.py (lines 186-193) builds its PATCH/POST body from ONLY description_html/state/labels and NEVER reads op["acceptance_criteria"]. RESULT (verified live): all 13 created NEW items carry only a rationale paragraph and NO acceptance criteria — they are oracle-free. The very items created to land the audit's CH-07 (located-oracle-before-done) and A-LOOP-01 (no-checkable-success-oracle) doctrine themselves have no checkable "done." This is the dominant, systemic ambiguity defect on the Phase-0 critical path (NEW-01/02/05/08/10 are priority:blocking). It is a sibling-class of GT-D1/D2/D3: the same applier that cannot create relations (GT-D2) and silently collapses a create onto a colliding match_key (GT-D1) also silently drops a whole declared field. Two concrete downstream consequences verified live: (a) NEW-05's COH-1 apply-step-0 hard gate, which the plan's own _meta.apply_step_0_gate says is "encoded as AC3 on NEW-05," is absent from the live item — the blocking pre-gate's oracle did not land; (b) the E7+E6-S5 status true-up (C16/S6/S7/S8), one plan op resolving to a single id (73e5a0ce=REQ16), moved only REQ16 to Backlog while E7-REQ6 (In-Verification), REQ27 (Human-Review), REQ11/REQ12 (Agent-Executing), REQ23 (Blocked, no blocker link), and E6-S5 (Done, no evidence) all still carry their outrun states — the report's "no item sits in a state its on-disk evidence cannot support" claim is false, and REQ23's Blocked-without-edge is an un-checkable terminal. Beyond the apply mechanics, several UPDATE notes hand the executor an imperative ("add the CH-10 rung", "make the check assert gates EXIST") rather than a checkable AC, leaving the "done" judgment to prose.

findings.title: All 13 created NEW items are oracle-free on the live board: applier drops the entire acceptance_criteria[] array
findings.evidence: plane-upsert-plan.json each create op carries a first-class acceptance_criteria[] array (NEW-01 has 5 ACs, NEW-05 4, NEW-06 4, NEW-14 5, etc.). apply_upsert_plan.py:186-193 builds `body` from ONLY description_html, state, labels — `op['acceptance_criteria']` is never referenced anywhere in the file. Live API confirms: GET work-items/{NEW-01..14} description_html contains the rationale paragraph but ZERO AC text (programmatic check: 'AC1' absent in all; AC-sentence substring match False for NEW-01/02/05/08/10/13). Cross-ref: report plane-reconciliation-report.md §5.1 presents these as 'verbatim-grade' ACs and §1 says COH-1 is 'encoded as a testable AC on the new A7 item (NEW-07)'.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM (report §5.1): NEW items carry load-bearing, verbatim-grade, testable acceptance criteria (full AC text 'in plane-upsert-plan.json'). REALITY: the AC text lives ONLY in the plan JSON; the live board items carry only a prose rationale and no acceptance criteria, so every NEW owning item — including the 5 priority:blocking Phase-0 items — has no mechanically checkable 'done.' This violates the audit's own CH-07 (located-oracle-before-done) and A-LOOP-01 (no-checkable-success-oracle) on the items created to enforce them.

recommended_action.detail: Re-run an applier that appends each op's acceptance_criteria[] into description_html (or a Plane sub-issue / AC field) for all 13 created items (NEW-01..11,13,14). Fix apply_upsert_plan.py to render acceptance_criteria into the PATCH/POST body. Priority order: NEW-05 (carries the COH-1 gate AC3), then the other priority:blocking items NEW-01/02/08/10.

findings.title: NEW-05's COH-1 apply-step-0 hard-gate oracle (AC3 CI assert) did not land — the blocking pre-gate is unenforceable on the board
findings.evidence: plane-upsert-plan.json _meta.apply_step_0_gate: 'COH-1 verifier-name reconciliation is the blocking pre-gate. It is encoded as AC3 on NEW-05.' NEW-05 plan AC3: 'a CI assert proves CLAUDE_AGENT_NAME resolves to the literal the gates compare against (verifier.md) across pre_tool_use_hook.py, subagent_stop_hook.py, and the two spine test fixtures — byte-for-byte.' Live NEW-05 (seq 254) description_html (619 chars) carries ONLY the option-(a) decision prose; the AC3 CI-assert oracle is absent (verified: 'assert'/'CI'/AC text not present). This AC3 is the runnable oracle for GT-7/COH-1 (actor literal verifier.md vs frontmatter name).
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: the COH-1 blocking gate is 'encoded as a testable AC on NEW-05' and is the apply-step-0 gate. REALITY: NEW-05 on the live board names the decision but carries no testable AC and no CI-assert predicate, so an executor reading the board cannot mechanically verify the byte-for-byte verifier.md convention before applying the actor gates — the exact fail-closed-but-stuck risk (GT-7) the gate exists to prevent.

recommended_action.detail: Append NEW-05 AC1-AC4 verbatim to its description, especially AC3 (the byte-for-byte CI assert over pre_tool_use_hook.py / subagent_stop_hook.py / test_subagent_stop_actor.py / test_pre_tool_use_authority.py). Mark NEW-05 as the apply-step-0 blocking gate in a checkable phrasing ('CI assert X passes' not 'reconcile the names').

findings.title: E7 + E6-S5 status true-up landed on only 1 of 6 stories: outrun states the report claims fixed still stand (incl. an un-checkable Blocked-without-edge)
findings.evidence: .apply_log.json final action 'E7 + E6-S5 status true-up (REQ16/REQ6/REQ27/REQ11/REQ23/REQ12 + E6-S5)' is a SINGLE update op with one id 73e5a0ce, which resolves to ASCP-E7-REQ16 only. findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM (report §6 View readiness, Workflow Kanban 'READY after apply'): 'E7 stories off Done/In-Verification (C16); E6-S5 off Done-without-evidence (S6); E7-REQ23 Blocked-without-link reconciled (S8) ... No item sits in a state its on-disk evidence cannot support.' findings.title: NEW-14 (board-integrity blocked-by edges) is undeliverable as built: the plan has zero relation ops and the applier cannot create relations — verified zero relations across the whole board
findings.evidence: plane-upsert-plan.json operations: 39 ops = 14 create + 25 update + 0 relation (OP TYPES {create, update}). apply_upsert_plan.py has no relation-creation code path (only PATCH work-items + POST module-issues/cycle-issues). Live relations endpoint returns all-empty buckets for E8-13, E4-S1, E5-S10, NEW-14 itself, NEW-01, NEW-02, REQ-INFRA-005 (blocked_by:0, blocking:0, relates_to:0, ...). findings.title: NEW-12 migration-authoring item has no owning record and its AC oracles are lost; REQ-INFRA-005 dangles a reference to a non-existent NEW-12
findings.evidence: Live: ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-12 ABSENT (only NEW-01..11,13,14 exist — 13 created, not 14). plane-upsert-plan.json NEW-12 has match_key 'story:REQ-INFRA-005' and is the ONLY create whose match_key collides with an existing item; apply_upsert_plan.py resolve() (lines 138-148) checks match_key FIRST, so the 'create' silently became an update to df8b5217=REQ-INFRA-005, then the later REQ-INFRA-005 UPDATE op (same id) clobbered it (GT-D1; .apply_log.json shows both ops on id df8b5217). NEW-12's 4 authored ACs (AC1 author 8 migration files; AC2 run_state carries all D3/D7 columns; AC3 gate_audit_log append-only grant; AC4 'eight migrations actually apply against a fresh DB' — a runnable oracle) are entirely lost. The testable AC4 ('migrations actually apply') vanished.

findings.title: Reconciliation UPDATE notes hand the executor an imperative instead of a checkable AC (E5-S10 'add the CH-10 rung'; E6-S7 'make the check assert gates EXIST')
findings.evidence: Live E5-S10 desc ends: '...omits the CH-10 escalate-don't-re-inject rung that directly closes C-LOOP-04 — add it.' — an instruction, with no oracle stating how 'rung added' is verified (no test name, no assert). Live E6-S7 desc: 'Make the check assert gates EXIST AND don't read Plane state.' — names the intent but no checkable predicate (e.g. which gate symbols must be importable). E6-S7's own task seq 182 still phrases the guard as 'no gate function imports or reads Plane state' (the vacuous-pass form C13 flags) — the non-vacuous oracle was described in prose but not encoded as a runnable assertion. E8-13 desc states the state move + ralph-disable intent but carries no post-apply verification oracle (the report's 5-point post-apply check from §7 is nowhere on the item).
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM (report §4.1 C8/C13): E5-S10 gets 'the CH-10 rung that closes C-LOOP-04'; E6-S7 is made 'non-vacuous (assert gates EXIST AND don't read Plane state).' REALITY: both land as prose imperatives to a future executor, not as mechanically-checkable acceptance criteria; the underlying tasks still carry the old vacuous phrasing. An executor cannot tell from the board when these are 'done' — exactly the vague-adjective / no-oracle pattern the audit (CH-07, A-LOOP-01) condemns.

recommended_action.detail: Rewrite the E5-S10 and E6-S7 reconciliation notes as testable ACs: E5-S10 'a property test proves the Nth identical Stop block escalates to a different message/HANDOFF, never byte-identical re-injection'; E6-S7 'a test asserts the gate modules (stop_hook/pre_tool_use/coverage_query) are importable AND a grep over them finds zero Plane-state reads — fails if gates are absent (non-vacuous).'
