findings.title: NEW-06's checkable teardown oracle is partly self-referential and shares the AO-01 AC-drop, leaving 'is it actually disabled?' unverifiable on the board
findings.evidence: NEW-06 plan ACs are genuinely checkable (AC1 'stop-hook.sh ABSENT from hook telemetry and governance Stop hook owns the event'; AC2 'flagd kill-switch OFF and no dispatch path live'; AC3 'a test proves a still-enabled plugin FAILS the oracle'). But (a) per AO-01 none of these ACs are on the live NEW-06 item (only rationale prose), and (b) AC1 defines the oracle as 'the same predicate UPD-E8-13 AC5 verifies' while E8-13 AC5 itself never landed as text (E8-13 live desc has no AC5 / no telemetry assertion) — the oracle points at another item's missing oracle. Report §3.1 finding table maps A-LOOP-01 to 'NEW-04 (doctrine) + NEW-06 (concrete oracle).'
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM (report §3.1/§5): A-LOOP-01 is RESOLVED via NEW-06, a 'concrete checkable oracle' for the disable/teardown scenario, distinct from NEW-04's doctrine. REALITY: NEW-06 on the board carries no AC (AO-01) and its strongest AC is defined by reference to E8-13 AC5, which also did not land — so the concrete teardown oracle for the very loop A-LOOP-01 names ('is it actually disabled?') is not checkable on the board. The doctrine→runnable-check promotion the report claims did not materialize.

recommended_action.detail: Land NEW-06 AC1-AC4 on the item AND make AC1 self-contained (state the telemetry assertion inline: 'hook-decision telemetry shows zero stop-hook.sh invocations and governance stop_hook.py owns Stop'), not by reference to E8-13 AC5. Cross-link NEW-06 <-> E8-13 and NEW-06 <-> E6-S2 via relates_to once relation support exists (AO-04).

finders.summary: The prior pass CLAIMED C-LOOP-04 (49x re-injection) and C-IDLE-03 (idle "standing by") RESOLVED via E5-S10, E8-21, E8-14, NEW-02, NEW-03 (report lines 122-123). The live board does not back this. Three independent, falsifiable failures keep the 49x loop and the idle stall PERMITTED as written: (1) the CH-10 escalate-don't-re-inject rung — the ONLY mechanism that stops byte-for-byte re-injection — is NOT IMPLEMENTED in any AC or subtask; E5-S10's live description (seq 37) literally still reads "ALSO omits the CH-10 escalate-don't-re-inject rung... — add it", i.e. the prior pass wrote the TODO into the description but never authored the fixing AC, and no E5-S10/E8-21 subtask implements a block_streak ladder. (2) The reentrancy guard, as specced on the board (E5-S10.1 seq 142, E8-21.3 seq 236), reads/writes a hook-owned run_state.stop_hook_active SELF-MUTEX, which is the WRONG mechanism: Claude Code sets stop_hook_active in the Stop EVENT payload (per proposed 02-stop_hook §1 is_reentrant(event)), not in run_state, so a guard keyed on run_state will never see the force-continuation and will not suppress re-injection. No board item asserts the load-bearing property "a Stop re-entry emits ZERO tokens." (3) HANDOFF-on-no-progress and external_blocker HANDOFF depend on the NEW-02 (D3) populator, but (a) NEW-02 has ZERO relations on the live board (verified via /relations/ API) so the dependency is prose-only, and (b) NEW-02's live description carries NONE of the report's ACs — including AC3, the single test "a 3-streak triggers HANDOFF, not re-injection" that the report stakes the whole C-LOOP-04 resolution on (report line 295). That AC, and every NEW-item AC, exists only in the report markdown — all four NEW items I checked live (NEW-01/03/10/13) have hasAC=False. Net: the anti-loop layer is specced in prose and non-executable as board items; as written every AC would STILL permit the 49x loop and the idle stall. Sibling of the GT-D2 class: the NEW-14 blocked-by edges (E5-S10/E8-14/E8-21 -> E8-13) and the NEW-02 -> consumer edges are all absent on the live board, so apply-order can schedule the consumers before the populator that makes their gates fire.

findings.title: CH-10 escalate-don't-re-inject rung never implemented; the 49x loop fix is a TODO left inside E5-S10's description
findings.evidence: Live E5-S10 (seq 37) description_html (fetched via API, updated 2026-06-18T17:39) reads verbatim: "ALSO omits the CH-10 escalate-don't-re-inject rung that directly closes C-LOOP-04 — add it." No E5-S10 subtask (142 evaluate_stop gate, 143 rego, 144, 145 Z3) implements escalation/block_streak/re-inject logic. E8-21 subtasks 234-238 implement HANDOFF-before-block ordering (234), lex-specialis tiebreak (235), reentrancy (236), Z3 fixtures (237), Plane mirror (238) — NONE implements the block_streak rung ladder. Board-wide grep for 'streak.*(handoff|re-inject)' or escalation-rung implementation returns 0 AC-bearing items. The proposed mechanism (02-stop_hook-steering.md §1 _escalate + _block_streak, §2.3 rung-0/rung-1/HANDOFF) is the ONLY thing that prevents byte-for-byte re-injection (charter CH-10, line 217; AP-3 line 392).
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report line 122 marks C-LOOP-04 RESOLVED via E5-S10+E8-21; report lines 179/440 say 'add the CH-10 rung that closes C-LOOP-04' to E5-S10. REALITY: the rung was never authored — E5-S10's live description still contains the instruction-to-add as unactioned finding text, and no subtask implements it. As written, the Stop hook would re-emit the identical block string every turn => the 49x loop survives.

recommended_action.detail: UPDATE story:ASCP-E5-S10 (and/or add a subtask) to replace the finding text with a concrete AC implementing the CH-10 escalation ladder per 02-stop_hook §1/§2.3: rung-0 locate+act, rung-1 acknowledge-repeat+redirect, rung>=BLOCK_ESCALATION_CAP -> HANDOFF; require a unit test asserting a repeated identical block escalates (string differs) then converts to HANDOFF, never re-injects byte-for-byte. Reconcile ownership with E8-21 (see TWL-04).

findings.title: Reentrancy guard specced on the wrong signal (run_state.stop_hook_active self-mutex) — will NOT suppress Claude Code's forced-continuation re-injection
findings.evidence: E5-S10.1 (seq 142) AC: "Implement with_reentrancy_guard(fn): set stop_hook_active=True; skip if already set; release only on allow." E8-21.3 (seq 236) AC: "with_reentrancy_guard(evaluate_stop) via run_state.stop_hook_active; release the flag only when the blocking condition clears." Both describe a hook-WRITTEN self-mutex on run_state. The proposed spec (02-stop_hook-steering.md lines 47-56, 292-296) reads event.get('stop_hook_active') from the STOP EVENT stdin (the field Claude Code sets on force-continue) and returns 0 before any gate. On-disk grep of .claude/hooks/stop_hook.py for stop_hook_active = 0 hits (guard is net-new). Claude Code never writes run_state.stop_hook_active; it writes the event payload, so a run_state-keyed guard cannot detect the re-entry it is supposed to short-circuit.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: E5-S10.1/E8-21.3 'implement the reentrancy guard' and the report (line 444) lists 'A Stop re-entry emits zero tokens' as a verification success criterion. REALITY: the board's guard reads the wrong field; it would never fire on a real forced continuation, so the hook still re-evaluates and re-injects. No board item asserts the zero-token re-entry property; E8-21.3's only test ('no cascade during a blocking cycle') tests the self-mutex, not event-level reentrancy.

recommended_action.detail: UPDATE task:ASCP-E8-21#2 (seq 236) and task:ASCP-E5-S10#0 (seq 142): the guard MUST read event['stop_hook_active'] from the Stop event payload as the FIRST action in main() and return exit 0 with zero stdout/stderr (per 02-stop_hook §1 is_reentrant + the order-of-operations block). Add an AC + unit test: a Stop event carrying stop_hook_active:true produces exit 0 and emits no steering tokens. Remove the run_state.stop_hook_active 'set/release-on-condition' self-mutex framing.

findings.title: NEW-02 (D3 populator) has zero relations and zero ACs on the live board — the HANDOFF/idle fix depends on it but the dependency and its load-bearing test exist only in prose
findings.evidence: Live /work-items/{NEW-02}/relations/ API returns all 8 buckets empty (blocking:0, blocked_by:0, relates_to:0). E8-21, E8-14, E5-S10, E8-13 relations are ALL empty too. NEW-02 live description (401 chars, fetched via API) carries the rationale blurb only — hasAC=False; it does NOT contain report §5 AC1 (counter list), AC2 (BLOCKED-ON sentinel parser), or AC3 ('a test proves a 3-streak triggers HANDOFF, not re-injection', report line 295). Verified hasAC=False on NEW-01/03/10/13 too — the report ACs never landed on any NEW item.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report line 123 marks C-IDLE-03 'RESOLVED (behavior live after D3)' and line 419 calls NEW-02 'the milestone that turns anti-loopmaxxing from inert to firing'; NEW-02 AC3 is the single test backing the C-LOOP-04 resolution. REALITY: NEW-02 is a prose stub with no ACs, no test, and no relation to any consumer. cap/budget/no-progress/external_blocker HANDOFF stay inert (counters never written), the idle 'standing by' loop is unfixed, and apply-order can schedule consumers (E8-14/21/E5-S10/E3-4/E4-S1/E6-S2/S3/S5) before the populator since no edge orders them.

recommended_action.detail: UPDATE ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-02 to carry report §5 ACs verbatim, especially AC3 (a 3-streak -> HANDOFF, not re-injection) and AC2 (BLOCKED-ON: <dependency> sentinel -> run_state.external_blocker). THEN add relations: NEW-02 BLOCKS E8-14, E8-21, story:ASCP-E5-S10, ASCP-E3-4, ASCP-E4-S1, E6-S2/S3/S5 (blocked_by edges to NEW-02), per report line 153. Use the live relations endpoint (apply_upsert_plan.py cannot create relations — GT-D2 class).

findings.title: Reentrancy + escalation-rung ownership is triply-claimed and self-contradictory across E5-S10, E8-14, E8-21
findings.evidence: E8-14 story (seq 56): 'E8-14 and E8-21 BOTH edit evaluate_stop (artifact-02 reentrancy + HANDOFF-before-block)' and report R5 (line 197) assigns 'E8-14 owns the reentrancy guard'. E8-21 story (seq 61): 'E8-21 owns the escalation/HANDOFF rungs inside evaluate_stop; E8-14 owns the reentrancy guard half.' BUT E8-21.3 (seq 236) is titled 'Implement the reentrancy guard composition' — placing the reentrancy guard under E8-21, contradicting both stories. AND E5-S10.1 (seq 142) ALSO implements with_reentrancy_guard. So three items implement/claim the reentrancy guard; meanwhile the escalation rung (TWL-01) is claimed by E8-21 yet E5-S10's desc is told to 'add the CH-10 rung'. R6 (line 198) tells E8-21 to 'cite the canonical HANDOFF contract (NEW-03/charter NEW-13) rather than re-stating the rungs' — but the block_streak rung is hook CODE (02-stop_hook §1), not citable prose in A8/charter, so citing them would leave the rung unimplemented.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: R5/R6 establish a clean scope contract (E8-14=reentrancy, E8-21=rungs, cite-don't-restate). REALITY: the board encodes the opposite — E8-21.3 implements reentrancy, E5-S10.1 also does, and the escalation rung has no implementing subtask anywhere; an executor reading the three items cannot tell which one builds the guard or the ladder, and 'cite the contract' misroutes the rung to a prose doc that does not contain the mechanism.

recommended_action.detail: UPDATE the three stories to a single non-overlapping contract: assign the event-level reentrancy guard to exactly ONE item (recommend E8-14 per R5) and the block_streak escalation ladder to exactly ONE item (E8-21), and rewrite E5-S10 to CONSUME both rather than re-implement. Clarify R6: the charter/A8 own the PRINCIPLE (CH-10) and the BLOCKED-ON sentinel, but the rung MECHANISM (block_streak ladder, _escalate template) is hook code owned by E8-21 — it must be implemented, not merely cited.

findings.title: Charter deliverable (NEW-13) mislabels escalate-don't-re-inject as CH-08; canonical clause is CH-10 — the loop items' citation target is mis-encoded
findings.evidence: audit/steering-audit/proposed/00-charter.md line 217 defines CH-10 = 'Vary or escalate on repeat; the escalation ladder is bounded (2-3 rungs -> HANDOFF)' and line 178 defines CH-08 = 'HANDOFF before block'. Report NEW-13 AC2 (line 336) says the charter encodes 'CH-08 (escalate-don't-re-inject)'. E5-S10's live description and report lines 102/179 correctly cite the doctrine as 'the CH-10 escalate-don't-re-inject rung'. So the charter-deliverable AC names the wrong clause for the exact doctrine the loop items reference.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: NEW-13 makes the loop items 'trace to a charter clause' (report line 337 references C-LOOP-04/CH-10). REALITY: NEW-13 AC2 binds escalate-don't-re-inject to CH-08, while every consumer cites CH-10 — the provenance link the charter is supposed to provide resolves to the wrong clause number, defeating the anti-drift purpose (R6).

recommended_action.detail: FIELD-EDIT/UPDATE the NEW-13 (seq 260) acceptance text and report NEW-13 AC2 to label escalate-don't-re-inject as CH-10 (matching charter line 217), and keep CH-08 = HANDOFF-before-block. Ensure E5-S10 and E8-21 cite CH-10 for the rung and CH-07 for reentrancy.

findings.title: E8-13 story claims it disables ralph-loop, but no E8-13 subtask or AC encodes the disable or the ralph-absent-from-telemetry test
findings.evidence: E8-13 story (seq 55): 'sets enabledPlugins."ralph-loop@…": false for governed sessions.' Subtasks: E8-13.1 (seq 211) registers six hooks + writes test_hook_config.py but does NOT mention ralph-loop disable; E8-13.2/3/4 (212/213/214) cover pre_tool_use guards, GitHub ruleset, exit-code property tests. None encodes the enabledPlugins disable or a test asserting ralph stop-hook.sh is absent from hook telemetry (report line 444 success criterion #5). GT-10/GT-1: ralph-loop's stop-hook.sh currently owns the Stop event and is the literal source of the 49x re-injection; disabling it is half the C-LOOP-04 fix (report line 72).
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report lines 102/105/120/122 credit E8-13 with 'the ralph disable' as part of resolving C-HOOK-01/C-LOOP-04. REALITY: no E8-13 subtask carries the disable as an AC or a verifiable test; the disable is asserted only in the story prose. If only hooks are registered but ralph is not disabled, Claude Code's multi-hook merge can still let the ralph Stop hook fire and re-inject (GT-10).

recommended_action.detail: UPDATE task:ASCP-E8-13#0 (seq 211) to add an AC: settings.json sets enabledPlugins['ralph-loop@...']=false for governed sessions, and test_hook_config.py (or a telemetry smoke test) asserts the ralph stop-hook.sh path never appears in Stop hook_events. Mirror report success criterion #5.

findings.title: NEW-10 PostToolUse forcing-function (fixes C-REDUND-06 rebuild/re-grep churn) is a prose stub with no AC and no relation to its extending stories
findings.evidence: NEW-10 live description (317 chars, API) is rationale-only, hasAC=False — it does not carry report §5 AC1 (one targeted check on the one changed file), AC2 (E4-S2/E4-S3 extend it), or AC3 (fails open). NEW-10 has no relation edges. C-REDUND-06 (04-transcript-forensics §2) = 12 Next.js rebuilds + 12 harness runs + 9 style checks + 11 read-before-write errors, whose convergence fix is exactly this single targeted-check PostToolUse hook (report line 313-318).
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report treats NEW-10 as the unified A6 hook that 'converges all three' redundancy findings (transcript-forensics Pattern 3). REALITY: as a board item it has no testable AC and no edges to E4-S2 (seq 270 PostToolUse handoff) or E4-S3; an executor cannot verify the 'one changed file, one located oracle' contract that prevents whole-suite re-runs, so the repeated-rebuild waste pattern is not provably prevented by the board as written.

recommended_action.detail: UPDATE ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-10 to carry report §5 AC1-AC3, and add relates_to/blocking edges to E4-S2 (seq 270) and E4-S3 so the 'both extend, neither re-registers' contract is enforceable. Add an AC asserting the hook runs ONE check on the ONE changed file and returns a located oracle + one corrective step (not a whole-suite re-run).

findings.title: NEW-03 (A8 goal directive) lacks the AC binding it to NEW-02's BLOCKED-ON parser, so the anti-idle sentinel channel is unverifiable end-to-end
findings.evidence: NEW-03 live description (523 chars, API) hasAC=False; it states the directive 'supplies the BLOCKED-ON sentinel that pairs with NEW-02' and 'forbids the agent from writing run_state directly' but carries no AC. The mechanism (08-goal-directive.md §3 + 02-stop_hook §4) is a two-ended contract: the agent emits 'BLOCKED-ON: <dependency>'; the NEW-02 driver parses it into run_state.external_blocker; the Stop hook HANDOFFs. NEW-03 and NEW-02 have no relation edge between them, and neither carries the matching AC.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report line 123 marks C-IDLE-03 RESOLVED via NEW-03+NEW-02; the A8<->A2 reconciliation is described in both items' prose. REALITY: the sentinel-emit (NEW-03) and sentinel-parse (NEW-02) halves are not bound by an AC or a relation, so the channel that converts an external blocker into a HANDOFF (instead of 24 idle 'standing by' turns) is not verifiable as a unit; a regression in either half would not be caught.

recommended_action.detail: Add a relates_to edge between ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-03 (seq 259) and ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-02 (seq 258), and UPDATE both to carry the matching AC: NEW-03 = agent ends stop summary with 'BLOCKED-ON: <dependency>' and never writes run_state; NEW-02 = parser maps that exact sentinel line to run_state.external_blocker. Add a paired integration test that the sentinel produces a HANDOFF, not a re-block.

finders.dimension: Hook generalization & dynamism — are the steering hooks/base-prompts generalized and dynamic enough, or brittle in ways that make Claude Code fight the hook instead of being steered, and does the board OWN making them dynamic?
finders.summary: The prior reconciliation pass correctly created owning items for the NEW hooks/artifacts (05 session_start→NEW-01, 06 post_tool_use→NEW-10, 07 agents→NEW-05, 08 goal→NEW-03, 00 charter→NEW-13, registry→E8-20) but systematically MISSED the generalization rewrites of the three hooks that ALREADY EXIST ON DISK IN BRITTLE FORM (pre_tool_use, subagent_stop, stop — artifacts 03/04/02). The deepening pass finds: (P0) NO board item owns the single highest-leverage dynamism fix — the phantom `tool_input["field"]` predicate in the live pre_tool_use_hook.py:38-44 that makes R1/R2 status/in_scope authority INERT on every real Edit/Write; the artifact-03 content-diff (`_changed_coverage_fields`) rewrite is unowned anywhere on 256 items. (P0) The live PreToolUse matcher is `Write|Edit|MultiEdit` (seq 211) — no `Bash` — leaving the protected-path anti-workaround bypass (`Bash`+`tee`, #63787) open, with no item carrying the CH-05 requirement to add `Bash`. (P0) The COH-1 fail-closed-but-stuck contradiction lives UNFIXED in subtask seq 131 which still says `name: verifier` (suffix-less), directly negating NEW-05's apply-step-0 `verifier.md` decision and re-creating the GT-7 failure on every proven-flip. (P1) Subtask seq 134 still encodes the forgeable `actor_agent`-from-payload role-separation block that fix #1 closes. (P1) EVERY claimed dependency edge (E8-13→hooks, E2-S2/E5-S10/E8-21→NEW-05/COH-1, NEW-02 consume/populate cross-links) is ABSENT as a live relation — GT-D2/D3 generalized across the whole graph: the dynamism-critical ordering (registry + agent frontmatter must precede the hooks' `verifier.md` compare) is invisible to any executor. (P2) Duplicate registry owner: both E8-20 (folded) and NEW-09 (its own desc says skip-unless-fold-rejected) are live, two items owning tools/execution_bounds — the single-source-of-truth the dynamism lever depends on, now split. The net effect: an executor working the board will wire the spine (E8-13) and leave pre_tool_use's authority gate silently inert, the matcher bypassable, and the actor literal mismatched — the precise conditions under which Claude Code self-corrects/patches the hook config instead of being steered by it.

findings.title: No board item owns the phantom-field→content-diff fix; live PreToolUse status/in_scope authority is inert on every real Edit/Write
findings.evidence: Live .claude/hooks/pre_tool_use_hook.py:38 reads `field = (tool_input or {}).get("field")` and lines 41/44 gate R1/R2 on `field == "status"` / `field == "in_scope"`. Real Edit payloads carry file_path/old_string/new_string and Write carries file_path/content — NEVER a `field` key (best-practices.md G6, charter AP-14), so the entire status/in_scope authority branch can never fire on a normal edit. Artifact 03 (03-pre_tool_use-steering.md §2) fixes this with `_changed_coverage_fields` diffing the JSON. A whole-board scan of all 256 snapshot items for `phantom|tool_input[|_changed_coverage|content-diff|old_string|new_string|field-based|inert on a real` returned NONE. The reorg routed R1/R2 only into UPD-E4-S2/UPD-E4-S3 whose live descs (seq 34/35) say 'EXTENDS the unified A6 hook (NEW-10)' — i.e. the WIRING/five-layer PostToolUse leg, not the PreToolUse authority predicate.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: plane-upsert-plan.json ops UPD-E4-S2/UPD-E4-S3 resolves:['R1','R2'] and §the report treat R1/R2 (the pre_tool_use field-authority strings) as handled by extending the unified hook. REALITY: R1/R2 in artifact 03 are the PreToolUse status/in_scope DENY predicates, whose load-bearing fix is the phantom-field→content-diff rewrite (G6, the #1 'High' mechanics gap). No board item — not E4-S2, not E4-S3, not E8-13 — carries an AC to replace `tool_input['field']` with a JSON delta. The most important dynamism fix in the whole audit is unowned; the gate stays silently false-green pre-write.

recommended_action.detail: Create a Phase-0 blocking story 'Generalize pre_tool_use_hook.py authority gate: phantom-field→content-diff (artifact 03)'. ACs: (1) replace `tool_input['field']` branch with `_changed_coverage_fields` diffing old_string/new_string (Edit) and on-disk-vs-content (Write); (2) COVERAGE_MODEL_PATHS + PROTECTED_PREFIXES read from tools/execution_bounds, literals as defaults; (3) human_signed resolved out-of-band via _resolve_human_signed, never from tool_input; (4) pin the R1-R5 verbatim catalog + the four AP-14 regression tests from artifact 03 §3. Mark resolves:['G6','AP-14','artifact:03-pre_tool_use-steering']. Block it by NEW-09/E8-20 (registry) and NEW-05 (COH-1). Owner: E4-S1 (implementer hook leg) is the closest existing home if you prefer update over create.

findings.title: Live PreToolUse matcher is Write|Edit|MultiEdit (no Bash) — the protected-path anti-workaround bypass is open and unowned
findings.evidence: E8-13 master registration subtask seq 211 (task:ASCP-E8-13#0) live desc: 'Register Stop, PreToolUse, PostToolUse (matcher Write|Edit|MultiEdit), SubagentStop, SessionStart, PreCompact as type:command'. Charter CH-05 and artifact 03 §1 R3 / best-practices G8 require the matcher to cover `Bash` so a `tee`/redirection write to a protected path (tests/, schema/, .claude/) cannot bypass the Edit|Write gate (verified failure #63787). Board-wide scan for `matcher…Bash | Edit|Write|Bash | Bash redirection` returned NONE across 256 items. The live pre_tool_use_hook.py R3 string (line 52) is also a bare verdict with no anti-workaround clause: 'protected artifact may not be edited by an agent: {path}'.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: UPD-E8-13 resolves the A-HOOK-01 wiring root and 'registers the six-hook spine' (report §3, op[0]). REALITY: the registration it specifies uses the brittle matcher that artifact 03 §5 explicitly flags as the bypass it must close ('ensure the matcher covers Bash, not only Edit|Write'). Wiring the spine as specified ships the #63787 bypass on day one; an agent told 'you may not edit tests/' reaches for Bash+tee and the matcher waves it through. No item owns adding Bash to the matcher or the R3 anti-workaround clause.

recommended_action.detail: Update story:ASCP-E8-13 (and subtask task:ASCP-E8-13#0): change the PreToolUse matcher to `Edit|Write|MultiEdit|Bash` and add an AC requiring the R3 deny string to carry the CH-05 anti-workaround clause ('Do NOT write it via Bash redirection, tee, or another tool — the same authority check applies') plus the named HANDOFF-to-main out. The hook must distinguish Bash-write-to-protected-path from benign Bash. Cross-reference artifact 03 R3.

findings.title: COH-1 fail-closed-but-stuck contradiction is UNFIXED on the live board: subtask still specifies suffix-less `name: verifier`
findings.evidence: Live subtask task:ASCP-E4-S3#0 (seq 131) desc: 'Write frontmatter (name: verifier, model: claude-opus-4-8, tools: [Read, Grep, Glob, Bash])'. The live hooks pin VERIFIER_AGENT='verifier.md' (pre_tool_use_hook.py:25, subagent_stop_hook.py:30) and actor_identity.py:44 resolves actor_agent from CLAUDE_AGENT_NAME. Per GT-7, a suffix-less `verifier` resolves to actor_agent=='verifier' != 'verifier.md', so the actor_not_verifier branch blocks EVERY legitimate proven-flip (fail-closed-but-stuck). NEW-05 (seq 254) correctly records the apply-step-0 decision 'name: verifier.md', but the contradicting subtask AC was never edited.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: NEW-05 resolves COH-1 and its desc states 'DECISION (apply-step-0): take option (a) name: verifier.md'. REALITY: the prior pass created the new owning item with the right convention but left the OLD contradicting instruction live in seq 131, which an implementer authoring verifier.md will follow verbatim — re-introducing the exact byte-mismatch COH-1 names. Two live items now give opposite frontmatter instructions; the brittle one is the buildable subtask.

recommended_action.detail: Update task:ASCP-E4-S3#0 (seq 131): change 'name: verifier' to 'name: verifier.md' and add a one-line note 'COH-1: the frontmatter name MUST be byte-identical to the hooks' VERIFIER_AGENT literal verifier.md; a suffix-less name fail-closes every proven-flip (GT-7).' Apply the same edit to any other subtask referencing `name: verifier`/`name: implementer` (check task:ASCP-E4-S2.* and E6-S4 dropdown values). Add a blocked-by edge from E4-S3 to NEW-05.

findings.title: SubagentStop role-separation subtask still keys the block on payload-supplied actor_agent (the forgeable pattern fix #1 closes) and uses bare-verdict exit-2 strings
findings.evidence: Live subtask task:ASCP-E4-S3#3 (seq 134) desc: 'Parse the Subagent-Result Envelope from stdin (actor_agent, requirement_id, omission_declaration…) … then role-separation block (actor_agent == implementer.md → exit 2)'. This reads actor_agent FROM the stdin envelope and blocks on a string compare. The live subagent_stop_hook.py:98 already resolves actor via resolve_identity (CLAUDE_AGENT_NAME) and artifact 04 §2 makes the located-defect+action catalog authoritative; both treat any payload actor_agent as untrusted. The subtask also specifies 'exit 2 naming missing fields' with no located+action string and no stop_hook_active reentrancy/escalation, i.e. the pre-artifact-04 brittle form.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: fix #1 (actor from runtime not payload) is implemented in the live subagent_stop_hook.py and artifact 04 is the canonical SubagentStop spec; UPD-E8-21 resolves the escalation/COH-3 leg. REALITY: the buildable subtask seq 134 still instructs an implementer to parse and trust actor_agent from the envelope and to emit bare-verdict exit-2 strings — directly contradicting the live hook and artifact 04. No item owns rewriting subagent_stop_hook.py's TEN bare-verdict reason strings to the artifact-04 located+action catalog, the stderr-on-exit-2 channel fix (G4), the stop_hook_active guard (G2), or the COH-3 approve→allow rename of the four lockstep tests.

recommended_action.detail: Update task:ASCP-E4-S3#3 (seq 134): replace 'Parse the Subagent-Result Envelope from stdin (actor_agent…) then role-separation block (actor_agent == implementer.md → exit 2)' with 'Resolve actor via tools/actor_identity (CLAUDE_AGENT_NAME); block when resolved_actor != verifier.md OR record.actor_agent != resolved_actor (artifact 04 fix #1). Emit located+action reasons to STDERR on exit 2; honor stop_hook_active.' Separately, widen UPD-E8-21 (seq 61) ACs to OWN the full artifact-04 string-catalog rewrite + channel fix + COH-3 approve→allow test update, not only the escalation rungs.

findings.title: Every claimed dependency/ordering edge for the dynamism chain is absent as a live relation (GT-D2/D3 generalized across the graph)
findings.evidence: GET /work-items/{id}/relations/ on all 13 key items (E8-13, E8-20, E8-21, E8-14, E2-S2, E4-S2, E4-S3, NEW-01/02/03/05/09/10) returns ALL buckets EMPTY (blocking/blocked_by/relates_to []). Yet the reorg notes CLAIM edges: E2-S2 'Add a hard dependency on NEW-05'; E8-21 'Add the NEW-02 dependency'; E8-14 'Cross-link both [NEW-02] so the consume/populate split is explicit'; E5-S10 names E8-13/COH-1 preconditions; NEW-02 'lands LAST' (an ordering edge). apply_upsert_plan.py has no relation-creation capability (GT-D2), so none landed.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM (GT-D2 was scoped to NEW-14→E8-13 only): the prior report and reorg notes describe a wired dependency graph — registry+frontmatter-before-hooks, populate-before-consume, COH-1-before-carve-out. REALITY: the dynamism ordering that makes the hooks fire correctly (tools/execution_bounds + verifier.md frontmatter MUST exist before the gates compare against the verifier.md literal; NEW-02 must populate block_streak/external_blocker before E8-21's escalation is non-inert) exists ONLY in prose buried in descriptions, invisible to any executor reading live blocked-by edges. This is the GT-D3 'collisions/omissions are a class' confirmed: the relation-omission is systemic, not the single NEW-14 case.

recommended_action.detail: Create the missing blocked_by edges (relation endpoints): E2-S2←NEW-05; E5-S10←NEW-05, E5-S10←E8-13, E5-S10←NEW-02; E8-21←NEW-02; E8-14↔NEW-02 (relates: column-vs-populate split); E4-S3←NEW-05 (COH-1, see HGD-3); HGD-1's new pre_tool_use story ←NEW-09/E8-20 and ←NEW-05; all Phase-0 hook stories ←E8-13 (wiring precondition). Because the applier cannot create relations, these must be added via POST /work-items/{id}/relations/ directly. Re-verify each with a GET afterward (do not trust the apply log).

findings.title: Duplicate owner of tools/execution_bounds: both E8-20 (folded) and NEW-09 (skip-unless-rejected) are live — the dynamism single-source-of-truth is split
findings.evidence: E8-20 (seq 60, Spec-Verified) live desc: 'Expand E8-20 to OWN authoring tools/execution_bounds (PREFERRED over a standalone item)'. NEW-09 (seq 256, Backlog) live desc: 'Conditional create. Only instantiate if UPD-E8-20 is NOT taken… Default-disabled: executor should prefer the E8-20 fold and skip this create unless that fold is rejected.' Both are live items. The registry holds VERIFIER_ROLE, BLOCK_ESCALATION_CAP, SPEC_COMPLETION_HARD_CAP, the Z3 timeout — the operator-overridable values every hook and base-prompt must read at runtime (charter CH-15, G6/G11 — hardcoded thresholds are the verified NON_DYNAMIC risk).
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: plan op NEW-09 is 'default-disabled; executor should prefer the E8-20 fold and skip this create' (its own machine instruction). REALITY: the fold (E8-20) WAS taken AND NEW-09 was still created — both survive, mirroring the GT-D1 collision class in reverse (there two ops collapsed and NEW-12 was lost; here the conditional should have collapsed and instead duplicated). Two items now own the registry that is supposed to be the single source the hooks and prompts both read, the exact 'second drifting source' CH-15/G11 forbid.

recommended_action.detail: Resolve the duplicate: since the E8-20 fold was taken, close/cancel NEW-09 (seq 256) as superseded-by-E8-20 (or convert it to a relates_to pointer), and ensure E8-20 is the sole owner of tools/execution_bounds. Add a note to E8-20 that VERIFIER_ROLE default must be byte-consistent with COH-1 ('verifier.md'). Verify no other item (E4-S3.1's 'coverage=85%', E2-S3's '7-check list') hardcodes a threshold the registry should own.

finders.summary: Verification pass against the LIVE board (256 items) + apply-log + upsert-plan. The prior pass's coverage is mostly real at the item-creation layer: 11 of the 13 staged artifacts/prereqs (A5/A6/A7/A8/A9/00-charter/D3/spec_validator/execution_bounds/D7 + NEW-14) DO have correctly-attached owning items (right module, cycle, phase, labels) — GT-11 holds. But three classes of claim≠reality survive and one new one is found. (1) GT-D1 is fully traced to a plan-authoring defect, not just the applier: NEW-12 is the ONLY create op carrying a non-null match_key (story:REQ-INFRA-005, identical to UPD-REQ-INFRA-005), so the upsert-by-match-key applier silently demoted the "create" to an "update" of df8b5217, which the later true-up then clobbered. Live board: exactly 13 audit-reorg items (NO NEW-12); REQ-INFRA-005's live description dangles "depend on NEW-12 authoring the migrations" at a non-existent item; the 8 .kiro migrations (E1's load-bearing work) have NO owning item. Plan _meta claims new:14/update:25; apply-log proves create:13/update:26. (2) GT-D2 generalizes to a total-relation-vacuum: E8-13 (A1 hub), NEW-14 itself, and every sampled consumer (E2-S1, E5-S10, NEW-02, E7-REQ23) have ALL relation buckets empty — the board has ZERO dependency edges. NEW-14's sole deliverable (relations-only, ~48 blocked-by edges) landed nothing; the §6 "Active Sprint/Roadmap READY-after-apply" claim that NEW-14 enforces spine-first ordering is false. (3) NEW (GT-D3 sibling, P1): the "E7 + E6-S5 status true-up (REQ16/6/27/11/23/12 + E6-S5)" was ONE op resolving to ONE id (E7-REQ16 only). 6 of 7 claimed true-ups never landed: E7-REQ6=In-Verification, REQ11/REQ12=Agent-Executing+human:review mismatch, REQ23=Blocked-with-no-link, REQ27=Human-Review, E6-S5=Done-with-no-evidence. The report §0/§4.1/§6 "Workflow Kanban READY — outrun states removed" is false on the live board; 14 stories total sit in outrun states (incl. E8-15/E8-19/E5-S17/S30/S32 never even enumerated). (4) NEW-09 and E8-20 BOTH own tools/execution_bounds (duplicate owner) though the report said NEW-09 should exist only if E8-20 stayed numerics-scoped — and E8-20 WAS expanded.

findings.title: GT-D1 root cause is the PLAN, not just the applier: NEW-12 is the only create with a colliding match_key; migration-authoring has no board home
findings.evidence: plane-upsert-plan.json operations[]: op id=NEW-12 has op:create, match_key:'story:REQ-INFRA-005', external_id:null — it is the ONLY one of 14 creates with a non-null match_key (the other 13 all have match_key:null). UPD-REQ-INFRA-005 carries the identical match_key. .apply_log.json shows NEW-12 recorded as op:update against id df8b5217 (not create), and df8b5217 is the only id hit by two update ops. Live API GET work-items/df8b5217 → name 'REQ-INFRA-005: Database Migration & Version Upgrade', external_id 'story:REQ-INFRA-005'. Snapshot: exactly 13 ascp-audit-reorg:* items (NEW-01..11,13,14); NEW-12 absent. REQ-INFRA-005 live desc_text: '…Reframe AC#4 to depend on NEW-12 authoring the migrations…' (dangling).
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM (report §1, §3.2, §5): 'creates 14 NEW owning items'; plan _meta.counts {new:14}; NEW-12 row exists in the §5 table as an E1/Phase-0/blocking owning item. REALITY: only 13 created (apply-log create:13/update:26); NEW-12 was never created — the upsert-by-match-key logic demoted the create to an update of REQ-INFRA-005, then UPD-REQ-INFRA-005 overwrote it. The eight .kiro migrations have no owning item; REQ-INFRA-005 points at a non-existent NEW-12.

recommended_action.detail: Create the missing item as external_id ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-12 (NOT by match_key): name 'Author eight .kiro migrations 001-008 + run_state completeness + gate_audit_log append-only grant', module E1, cycle Phase 0, labels phase:0/type:wiring/priority:blocking, state Backlog. Port AC1-AC4 from report §5.1. Root-cause fix on the applier: forbid op:create from carrying a match_key (or treat a create whose match_key resolves to an existing item as a hard error, not a silent update). Then field-edit REQ-INFRA-005 so its dangling 'NEW-12' reference resolves to the real item.

findings.title: Total relation vacuum: NEW-14's blocked-by topology never landed (GT-D2 generalized to the whole board)
findings.evidence: Live API /work-items/{id}/relations/ for E8-13 (110f71c8), NEW-14 (82f43d5b), E2-S1 (bf3e17e3), E5-S10 (76e1a1a6), NEW-02 (80d42539), E7-REQ23 — ALL return every bucket = 0 (blocking/blocked_by/relates_to/start_after/…). NEW-14 AC1/AC2 require ~24 hook-consuming stories to carry blocked-by edges to E8-13 + the ralph-disable; AC-NOTE 'relations only — no story content/state changes'. apply_upsert_plan.py PATCHes only description_html/state/labels (no relation API call exists in the tool — GT-D2).
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM (report §2 cross-epic, §5.1 NEW-14, §6 Active Sprint/Roadmap): NEW-14 'turns that assumption into enforced board topology (explicit blocked-by edges) so apply order cannot schedule a hook-consuming story ahead of the registration'; views 'READY after apply'. REALITY: zero relations exist anywhere on the board; NEW-14 is a story DESCRIBING edges that do not exist. Nothing enforces spine-first ordering; Active Sprint/Roadmap dependency-ordering claims are unsupported.

recommended_action.detail: Add blocked_by edges via POST /work-items/{id}/relations/ from each hook-consuming story (E2-S1/S2/S3, E3-1/-2/-4/-22, E4-S1/S2/S3/S4, E5-S10, E6-S2/S3/S7, E7-REQ6/11/12/16/23/27, E8-14/18/21/26) to E8-13 (110f71c8). Extend the applier with relation-creation support (it currently cannot create edges). Until edges exist, mark NEW-14 as NOT-DONE rather than Spec-Verified.

findings.title: 6 of 7 claimed E7/E6-S5 status true-ups never landed — outrun states persist (GT-D3 sibling: one op, one id)
findings.evidence: apply-log op 'E7 + E6-S5 status true-up (REQ16/REQ6/REQ27/REQ11/REQ23/REQ12 + E6-S5)' has a SINGLE id 73e5a0ce = E7-REQ16 (the applier patches one item per op). Live snapshot states: E7-REQ6=In-Verification, E7-REQ11=Agent-Executing (+human:review), E7-REQ12=Agent-Executing (+human:review, gate:stop), E7-REQ23=Blocked, E7-REQ27=Human-Review, E6-S5=Done (completed_at 2026-06-17). Only E7-REQ16=Backlog. Live relations for E7-REQ23 (Blocked): all buckets 0 (S8 dead-end Blocked, unfixed).
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM (report §0 Headline lists 'Stories in Done/In-Verification/Agent-Executing — status outruns reality' as fixed; §4.1 C16 'true-up status to Backlog/Spec-Verified'; §4.4 S6/S7/S8; §6 'Workflow Kanban READY after apply — no item sits in a state its on-disk evidence cannot support'). REALITY: 6 of the 7 named items keep their outrun states; the agent_role:human + Agent-Executing mismatch (S7) and the Blocked-without-link (S8) and Done-without-evidence (S6) are all UNFIXED. The Workflow-Kanban readiness claim is false.

recommended_action.detail: Split the one true-up op into per-item field-edits: set E7-REQ6/REQ11/REQ12/REQ27 → Backlog (or Spec-Verified), E6-S5 → Backlog (off Done; no in-slice evidence), and either encode E7-REQ23's blocker relation (prereq NEW-11/migrations) or move it off Blocked. Reconcile the agent_role:human ↔ Agent-Executing mismatch on REQ11/REQ12.

findings.title: Outrun-state defect is broader than the report enumerated: E8-15/E8-19/E5-S17/S30/S32 never targeted for true-up
findings.evidence: Snapshot: E8-15=Agent-Executing ('Inner-Loop Orchestration'), E8-19=Agent-Executing ('Predictive Routing'), E5-S17=In-Verification ('Supply-Chain & Security Gates'), E5-S30=Human-Review ('DeepEval'), E5-S32=Human-Review ('Kill-Switch'), E6-S6=In-Verification ('Integration observability'). apply-log: none of these ids appears in any update op (touched_by_update=False for all 14 advanced-state stories). On-disk GT-1..6: settings.json, agents, migrations, the consuming tools are all ABSENT, so every Agent-Executing/In-Verification claim outruns reality.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM (report §0): the reconciliation 'true-ups outrun statuses'. REALITY: the true-up scope was limited to the 6 E7/E6-S5 names (which themselves didn't land — COV-03); 8 further stories in equally-unsupported advanced states were never enumerated and remain outrun. E8-15 Agent-Executing is especially load-bearing — it is the inner-loop orchestration that cannot be 'executing' while .claude/settings.json + agents are absent.

recommended_action.detail: Extend the status-true-up to E8-15, E8-19, E5-S17, E5-S30, E5-S32, E6-S6: move each to Backlog/Spec-Verified consistent with on-disk evidence. Add a board-integrity invariant: no story may sit in a started/completed/cancelled state-group while its named on-disk artifact (hook/tool/migration) is absent.

findings.title: NEW-09 and E8-20 both own tools/execution_bounds — duplicate owning items contradict the report's conditional-create rule
findings.evidence: apply-log: BOTH an update op on E8-20 (id 3d0b588f, desc 'Expand E8-20 to OWN authoring tools/execution_bounds (PREFERRED over a standalone item)') AND a create op for NEW-09 (desc 'Conditional create. Only instantiate if UPD-E8-20 is NOT taken'). Live snapshot: NEW-09 exists (seq256, Backlog, E3) AND E8-20 carries the expanded ownership desc. Two live items now both author the same module.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM (report §3.3, §5 table 'NEW-09 (prefer tightening E8-20)', NEW-09 desc 'Only instantiate if UPD-E8-20 is NOT taken'). REALITY: E8-20 WAS taken/expanded yet NEW-09 was still created unconditionally (the applier has no conditional-create logic). Result is a redundant duplicate owner for tools/execution_bounds — the §8 'no duplicate ownership' posture is violated for this prereq.

recommended_action.detail: Close/cancel NEW-09 (or relabel it as superseded-by-E8-20) since E8-20 now owns execution_bounds. Alternatively delete NEW-09's content and convert it to a relates_to pointer to E8-20. Record in E8-20 that the standalone NEW-09 fold was rejected so the duplicate is intentional-or-removed, not silently dangling.

findings.title: PreCompact (D7) is owned by both NEW-11 and an existing E7-REQ11 task — overlapping coverage with no scope contract
findings.evidence: NEW-11 (seq262, E7, Phase 2, Backlog) = 'PreCompact resume_state_hash checkpoint hook (D7)'. But existing task ASCP-E7-REQ11.3 (seq191, digest line 525) = 'pre_compact_hook.py checkpoint writer + baseline-hash producer … Write durable run_state.resume_state_hash'. Both author the same PreCompact checkpoint + resume_state_hash producer. The report created NEW-11 as the owning item but left E7-REQ11.3 unreconciled (E7-REQ11 was not touched by any update op — see COV-03/04).
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM (report §3.3 / §5: NEW-11 is THE owning item for D7 PreCompact; 'no owning item' was the gap it closes). REALITY: an owning task already existed under E7-REQ11 (task .3). NEW-11 duplicates it without a scope contract (unlike R5's E8-14/E8-21 reconciliation), so two items claim the PreCompact hook — the same split-ownership pattern the report fixed elsewhere but missed here.

recommended_action.detail: Add a relates_to / scope-contract note between NEW-11 (owns the unified D7 hook) and E7-REQ11.3 (extends it), mirroring the R5 E8-14/E8-21 treatment, so the two don't independently re-author pre_compact_hook.py. Reference NEW-11 from E7-REQ11.3's description.

finders.summary: Re-verified the prior pass's 16 contradiction fixes (C1-C16) against LIVE item descriptions via the Plane API and the snapshot. The PROSE half of most fixes held: C1 (E8-13 off Agent-Executing, now Spec-Verified), C3 (E2-S3 deepeval removed), C5 (E3-* precondition blocks), C9/S1 (E5-S31 cleanly Phase-4, single cycle), C10/C11/C13/C14 (E6-S2/S3/S7/E8-18 deferred-leg notes) are all present verbatim in the live description_html. BUT three systemic defects mean large parts of the claimed reconciliation never landed on the board, all siblings of the GT-D1/D2/D3 apply-mechanism class: (1) GT-D1 root cause pinned and blast radius widened — NEW-12 was authored as op:create but with match_key=story:REQ-INFRA-005, so the applier's resolve() treated it as an UPDATE of REQ-INFRA-005 and the separate REQ-INFRA-005 true-up op then clobbered it; NEW-12 exists on zero items yet is dangling-referenced by FOUR live items (REQ-INFRA-005 seq20, E3-1 seq27, E3-2 seq28, E7-REQ16 seq54), so the plan's _meta.counts.new=14 is false (13 created). (2) C16 is the worst miss: the single UPD-E7-STATUS op declared 7 targets via an additional_match_keys field the applier never reads (apply_upsert_plan.py resolve() only consumes match_key/target_name), so only E7-REQ16 was trued-up to Backlog while E7-REQ6 (In-Verification), E7-REQ27 (Human-Review), E7-REQ11 (Agent-Executing), E7-REQ23 (Blocked, no blocker edge), E7-REQ12 (Agent-Executing) and E6-S5 (Done) ALL still carry states that outrun absent on-disk tools/migrations — the exact contradiction C16 claimed resolved, left live in 6 of 7 items. (3) NEW class — every one of the 39 ops carries a rich acceptance_criteria[] array, but the applier PATCHes only description_html/state/labels, so all 25 UPD ACs and 10/13 NEW-item ACs (incl. E8-13's AC1 'exactly five events', AC5 ralph Stop-ownership telemetry, AC6 SPINE_REQUIRED_EVENTS scoping) were never written to the board. Plus a parent/child internal contradiction the prior pass introduced-but-missed: E8-13's rewritten parent wires 5 events and omits PreCompact (per COH-2), while its untouched child task ASCP-E8-13.1 (seq 211) still says register 6 events including PreCompact pointing at an unauthored D7 hook. GT-D2 re-verified live: E8-13, NEW-14, and every hook-consumer (E4-S1, E5-S10, E6-S2, E7-REQ12, E3-1) have ALL relation buckets empty.

findings.title: C16 true-up landed on 1 of 7 items — applier silently ignores additional_match_keys; 6 E7/E6 stories still carry states that outrun reality
findings.evidence: plane-upsert-plan.json op UPD-E7-STATUS declares match_key=story:ASCP-E7-REQ16 PLUS additional_match_keys=[REQ6,REQ27,REQ11,REQ23,REQ12,E6-S5] (7 targets). apply_upsert_plan.py:138-148 resolve() reads ONLY op['match_key'] and op['target_name'] — additional_match_keys is never referenced. .apply_log.json has ONE update action id=73e5a0ce (=REQ16). Live/snapshot states: REQ16=Backlog (fixed), but REQ6=In-Verification, REQ27=Human-Review, REQ11=Agent-Executing, REQ23=Blocked, REQ12=Agent-Executing, E6-S5=Done — all unchanged. REQ16's own live desc enumerates the exact contradiction left unfixed in its 6 siblings: 'E7 stories carry advanced states (Done, In-Verification, Human-Review, Agent-Executing, Blocked) while their underlying tools/migrations/telemetry are absent... True-up EACH'. Per remediation 05 §5.2, D3/D7 (the prereqs these tools need) are NOT AUTHORED.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report §4.1 C16 + §4.4 S6/S7/S8 + Workflow-Kanban view 'READY after apply' state that all six E7 stories + E6-S5 are trued-up to Backlog/Spec-Verified so 'no item sits in a state its on-disk evidence cannot support'. REALITY: only REQ16 moved; the other 6 live items still sit in In-Verification/Human-Review/Agent-Executing/Blocked/Done with zero supporting on-disk artifact. The Kanban 'no outrun state' guarantee is false for 6 of 7 targeted items.

recommended_action.detail: Re-run the true-up against the 6 missed targets individually: set story:ASCP-E7-REQ6, REQ27, REQ12 -> Spec-Verified (or Backlog); story:ASCP-E7-REQ11 -> Backlog and resolve its agent_role:human+Agent-Executing mismatch (S7); story:ASCP-E7-REQ23 -> Backlog (S8: it is Blocked with no blocker edge); story:E6-S5 -> Spec-Verified/Backlog (S6: Done with no evidence). Separately, fix apply_upsert_plan.py resolve() to iterate match_key + additional_match_keys so fan-out ops apply to every declared target.

findings.title: All 39 ops authored acceptance_criteria the applier never writes — 25 UPD + 10/13 NEW items carry only a prose contradiction-note on the live board, not their testable ACs
findings.evidence: All 39 entries in plane-upsert-plan.json carry a non-empty acceptance_criteria[] array (verified: 39/39). apply_upsert_plan.py:186-217 builds the PATCH/CREATE body from ONLY description_html, state, labels — acceptance_criteria is never serialized into any request. .apply_log.json confirms every action's fields=[description_html,state,labels]. Live snapshot: 10 of 13 NEW items have NO 'ACn' markers in desc_text (NEW-01/02/03/04/05/08/09/10/11/13); the E8-13 op carries 6 ACs (AC1 'registers exactly five governance events', AC5 'ralph Stop-ownership telemetry... stop-hook.sh ABSENT', AC6 'SPINE_REQUIRED_EVENTS scoped to five') yet E8-13's live desc is only the 4-sentence C1 note with none of them.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report §5.1 presents 'the load-bearing few, verbatim-grade' ACs (NEW-01..NEW-14, E8-13 AC5 ralph telemetry) as the deliverable, and Milestone-6 asserts the 'E8-13 (UPD) ralph Stop-ownership telemetry AC' was added. REALITY: those ACs exist ONLY in plane-upsert-plan.json; the live board carries none of them. An executor reading the board sees a 3-5 sentence note, not the testable acceptance criteria the report's coverage matrices treat as landed.

recommended_action.detail: Either (a) extend the applier to write acceptance_criteria into the item (Plane supports it via a custom field or by appending an '## Acceptance Criteria' block to description_html), or (b) re-PATCH each item's description_html to append its planned ACs. Highest priority: ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-05 (COH-1 byte-consistency AC3), NEW-02 (D3 counter list AC1), NEW-08 (validate_spec verdict AC1), and story:ASCP-E8-13 (AC1 five-events, AC5 ralph telemetry, AC6 SPINE_REQUIRED_EVENTS) — these are the spine-critical testable contracts currently invisible on the board.

findings.title: GT-D1 root cause pinned and blast radius widened: NEW-12 op carries a match_key so it could never create; dangling-referenced by 4 live items, not 1
findings.evidence: plane-upsert-plan.json op id=NEW-12 has op='create' BUT match_key='story:REQ-INFRA-005'. apply_upsert_plan.py:195-196 calls resolve(op) first; since match_key resolves to the existing REQ-INFRA-005 (df8b5217), the create is executed as an UPDATE of that item, then the separate REQ-INFRA-005 true-up op (also -> df8b5217) clobbers it (.apply_log: two update actions on df8b5217). Live: zero items have NEW-12 in external_id or name (verified). NEW-12 is dangling-referenced in the desc_text of FOUR live items: REQ-INFRA-005 ('depend on NEW-12 authoring the migrations'), E3-1 and E3-2 ('execution-ready only after NEW-08 + NEW-12 + spine'), and E7-REQ16. plan _meta.counts={new:14} but apply created 13.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report §3.2/§5 + plan _meta.counts assert 14 NEW owning items created incl. NEW-12 (E1 migration authoring). GT-D1 already flagged NEW-12 missing but scoped the dangling reference to REQ-INFRA-005 only. REALITY: NEW-12 was never creatable (its op had a match_key), only 13 items exist, and the dangling pointer misleads 4 execution-ready preconditions — E3-1/E3-2 are gated on an owning item that can never be reached.

recommended_action.detail: Create the standalone NEW-12 item (E1, Phase 0, priority:blocking, external_id ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-12) owning 'author eight .kiro migrations 001-008 + run_state completeness + gate_audit_log append-only grant' with the AC1-AC4 from report §5.1. Then the NEW-12 references in REQ-INFRA-005, E3-1, E3-2, E7-REQ16 resolve. Root-cause fix: remove match_key from any plan op whose op=='create', or make the applier treat op=='create' as create-only regardless of match_key.

findings.title: NEW-14 is Spec-Verified but its sole deliverable (the ~24 blocked-by edges) landed zero relations — every hook-consumer + E8-13 have empty relation buckets (GT-D2 verified live and broadened)
findings.evidence: Live GET /work-items/{id}/relations/ returns ALL buckets empty for: NEW-14 (id 82f43d5b), ASCP-E8-13 (110f71c8), ASCP-E4-S1 (dc91bfb3), ASCP-E5-S10 (76e1a1a6), E6-S2 (c545b37d), E7-REQ12 (51779832), E3-1 (de6c6a7c). apply_upsert_plan.py has no relation-creation path (no POST to /relations/; only work-items PATCH + module/cycle assign). NEW-14 op AC1/AC2/AC3 require blocked-by edges from ~24 stories to E8-13 + the ralph-disable leg; none exist. NEW-14's live state is Spec-Verified.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report §2 cross-epic note + Active-Sprint/Roadmap views assert NEW-14 'turns that assumption into enforced board topology (explicit blocked-by edges) so apply order cannot schedule a hook-consuming story ahead of the registration', and NEW-14 sits Spec-Verified as if delivered. REALITY: the applier physically cannot create relations; NEW-14 delivered zero edges. Its three ACs are unsatisfiable by the tool that 'applied' it, and the spine-first ordering guarantee does not exist on the board.

recommended_action.detail: Add blocked_by relations: from each of E2-S1/S2/S3, E3-1/-2/-4/-22, E4-S1/S2/S3/S4, E5-S10, E6-S2/S3/S7, E7-REQ6/REQ11/REQ12/REQ16/REQ23/REQ27, E8-14/18/21/26 -> ASCP-E8-13 (id 110f71c8) via POST /work-items/{consumer}/relations/ with relation_type blocked_by. Until the edges exist, move NEW-14 off Spec-Verified to Backlog so its state does not assert un-delivered work.

findings.title: E8-13 parent/child PreCompact contradiction introduced-but-missed: parent rewrite wires 5 events (no PreCompact) while child task ASCP-E8-13.1 still says register 6 incl. PreCompact, pointing at an unauthored D7 hook
findings.evidence: Live E8-13 parent desc (rewritten by C1 fix): 'Wires SessionStart / PreToolUse / PostToolUse / SubagentStop / Stop' = 5 events, PreCompact omitted. E8-13 op AC1 (in plan, unwritten) reinforces 'exactly five governance events'. Child task ASCP-E8-13.1 (seq 211, untouched by prior pass) desc_text: 'Register Stop, PreToolUse, PostToolUse (matcher Write|Edit|MultiEdit), SubagentStop, SessionStart, PreCompact as type:command (task 9.1)... Write tests/smoke/test_hook_config.py asserting all hooks are command-type'. Remediation 05 §3.2 COH-2 + §5.2 D7 mark PreCompact as deliberately NOT registered / NOT AUTHORED. GT-3: no pre_compact_hook.py exists on disk.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: report C1 says E8-13 is reconciled to a coherent 5-event registration; COH-2 scopes PreCompact out by design. REALITY: the prior pass fixed the parent prose but left the child task #0 instructing the executor to register a 6th (PreCompact) command and to write a smoke test asserting all registered hooks are command-type — which will fail or reference a hook (D7) that does not exist. Parent and child now contradict each other on the event count.

recommended_action.detail: Edit task:ASCP-E8-13#0 (seq 211) to register exactly five events (drop PreCompact) and note PreCompact is deferred to NEW-11 (D7); align the smoke test wording to assert the five registered hooks only. Add a relates_to edge from task ASCP-E8-13#0 to NEW-11 so the deferred 6th event is tracked, not lost.

findings.title: Phase-0 spec-completion items sit Spec-Verified while their load-bearing prereqs are NOT AUTHORED per the audit's own residual-gap ledger — a softer outrun-state contradiction the prior pass left unflagged
findings.evidence: Live states: E5-S10=Spec-Verified, E8-21=Spec-Verified, E3-4=Spec-Compiling, NEW-02(D3 populator)=Spec-Verified. Remediation 05 §5.2/§7.1 explicitly lists the loop driver/run_state populator (D3) and tools/spec_validator.py as NOT AUTHORED; the report itself (§1, §5.1) states 'cap/budget/no-progress->HANDOFF is inert until the D3 run_state populator (NEW-02) writes the counters'. E5-S10/E8-21/E3-4 all CONSUME those counters (their descriptions add the NEW-02 dependency) yet sit in Spec-Verified, and NEW-02 — the unwritten populator — is itself Spec-Verified.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM: Workflow-Kanban view asserts 'no item sits in a state its on-disk evidence cannot support' after apply. REALITY: Spec-Verified asserts the spec is verified/ready, but these specs depend on a populator (D3/NEW-02) and validator (NEW-08) the audit classes as not-built; the C16 'state must reflect on-disk reality' principle the prior pass applied to E7 was not applied to these Phase-0 consumers or to NEW-02 itself.

recommended_action.detail: Flag for awareness: this is a defensible posture (Spec-Verified = the SPEC is verified, not the implementation) but it is inconsistent with the C16 rule the prior pass enforced on E7. If consistency is wanted, either keep these Spec-Verified and document that Spec-Verified means spec-only (add a note), or true-up NEW-02/NEW-08 to Backlog until authored. Decision should be recorded once and applied uniformly.

findings.title: R5 GT-D3 E8-14/E8-21 evaluate_stop scope contract prose-only, no relation; and acceptance_criteria is a phantom field so all R1/R5/R6 ACs were dropped
findings.evidence: Live empty relation buckets on E8-14/56, E8-21/61, NEW-10/253, E4-S2/34, E4-S3/35, NEW-03/259, NEW-13/260, NEW-14/251. .apply_log.json fields are description_html,state,labels for all 39 ops; Plane has no acceptance_criteria field, so the R5 scope contract (UPD-E8-14/E8-21 AC2), R6 HANDOFF citation (NEW-03 AC6), R1 extend-not-re-register (NEW-10 AC2) and COH-1 VERIFIER_ROLE (E8-20 AC3) were all dropped. Created dups: NEW-09/256 + E8-20/60 both own execution_bounds; NEW-11/262 dups E7-REQ11.3/191.
findings.claim_vs_reality: CLAIM R1/R5/R6 resolved via scope contracts, cross-links and cite-by-id. REALITY all prose-only with zero relations and zero persisted ACs (GT-D2/D3 sibling); NEW-14 itself has zero of ~48 promised edges. KEEP R4/R6 defense-in-depth.

recommended_action.detail: Fix apply_upsert_plan.py to render acceptance_criteria into description_html AND create relations; then add relates_to E8-14/E8-21 (R5), blocked_by E4-S2/E4-S3 to NEW-10 (R1), relates_to the 5 layers to NEW-03 (R6), NEW-14 blocked_by edges to E8-13, cancel NEW-09, relates_to NEW-11 to E7-REQ11, and PATCH E6-S5 off Done.

verified.title: C16/S6/S7/S8 state true-up landed on only 1 of 7 targets — six additional_match_keys silently dropped (sibling of GT-D1/D3)
verified.note:
INDEPENDENTLY CONFIRMED on the live board 2026-06-18. Tried to refute and could not. Live GET /work-items/: REQ16 (73e5a0ce)=Backlog, updated_at=2026-06-18T17:40:56, and its description_html carries the full C16 true-up text ('status claims outrun on-disk reality'). The other six are ALL untouched: REQ6=In-Verification, REQ27=Human-Review, REQ11=Agent-Executing, REQ23=Blocked, REQ12=Agent-Executing, E6-S5=Done — every one updated_at=2026-06-17T22:32 (original creation stamp) and every one still carries its original '

EARS requirements' description with C16-text=False (verified by string search on live description_html). apply_log records UPD-E7-STATUS as ONE action against the single id 73e5a0ce; upsert-plan operations[38] lists match_key=story:ASCP-E7-REQ16 + additional_match_keys=[REQ6,REQ27,REQ11,REQ23,REQ12,E6-S5]. The applier resolved only the primary match_key. Exactly the GT-D1/D3 collision class (additional_match_keys are not iterated). 1/7 = ~14% applied. Severity P0 upheld: this is the spine's state-honesty milestone and 86% of it never landed.

recommended_action.detail: Issue six independent state PATCHes to Backlog (state id d83bd05d-f8a2-4440-ad90-00596609af6c): REQ6, REQ27, REQ11, REQ23, REQ12, E6-S5 (resolve each external_id to its live id via /work-items/ — note GT-D2's a120af4f for E8-13 is stale; resolve fresh), and re-apply the C16 true-up description_html to each of the six. ROOT-CAUSE fix in apply_upsert_plan.py: fan out additional_match_keys into N independent resolve+PATCH calls, emitting one apply_log action per resolved id, so a multi-target op can never collapse to a single id again. Then read-back-verify each (GT-D3 closure).

If Blocked is retained, add a blocked_by edge via POST /work-items/09176986-91b3-4618-a233-85e90c40c6d0/relations/ with relation_type=blocked_by to a REAL existing prereq: NEW-11 (PreCompact resume_state_hash, ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-11 — created in the prior apply per apply_log) and/or REQ-INFRA-005 (df8b5217) as the migration owner. Live state REQ11=Agent-Executing, REQ12=Agent-Executing (both group=started, state id b7b5ff8a), both updated_at=2026-06-17T22:32 (untouched). board-digest labels confirm REQ11=[...,human:review,...] and REQ12=[...,gate:stop,human:review,...] (digest seq 50/51). verified.note: CONFIRMED live by direct GET of each story. E6-S6=In-Verification (integration OTel spans; telemetry absent), ASCP-E8-29=In-Verification (omission-declaration gate; subagent_stop hook/schema absent GT-3/GT-2), ASCP-E8-26=In-Verification (reasoning-loop detection; telemetry absent), ASCP-E8-19=Agent-Executing (predictive routing), ASCP-E8-15=Agent-Executing (inner-loop orchestration — spine unwired per GT-1, so nothing can be agent-executing). verified.title: Reentrancy guard (seq 236) reads stop_hook_active from the run_state DB row, but the runtime delivers it on Stop-event STDIN; stop_hook.py never reads it and with_reentrancy_guard is unimplemented
verified.note: CONFIRMED on live board + on disk. Live work-item 7b259aa5 (seq 236, task:ASCP-E8-21#2) NAME='ASCP-E8-21.3 Implement the reentrancy guard composition', live desc='with_reentrancy_guard(evaluate_stop) via run_state.stop_hook_active; release the flag only when the blocking condition clears...'. On disk: grep for 'with_reentrancy_guard' and 'stop_hook_active' across .claude/hooks/ and tools/ returns ZERO matches (exit 1) — the guard is unimplemented. stop_hook.py main() reads ONLY event.get('run_state') (L211) and event.get('feature_list') (L212) from stdin; never reads the top-level stop_hook_active boolean the runtime delivers per code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks. Seq 189 (E7-REQ11.1) and seq 217 (E8-14.3) add stop_hook_active as a run_state DB column populated by NEW-02's loop driver (seq 258), not by the runtime. Live relations on seq 236 AND seq 189 are ALL EMPTY (blocked_by/blocking/relates_to=0), so the recommended relates_to edge does not yet exist (consistent with GT-D2). Keying the anti-loop guard off a DB column the runtime never sets means a persistently-unproven item still blocks to the runtime block-cap and is force-overridden (#63674). Claim (DB-column gate) != reality (stdin flag authoritative). P1 upheld. Evidence: live seq 236 desc; stop_hook.py:211-212; grep zero hits; digest seq 189/520; empty relations on 236 & 189.

recommended_action.detail: Amend seq 236 (task:ASCP-E8-21#2) AC: 'The reentrancy guard MUST read the top-level stop_hook_active boolean from the Stop/SubagentStop event STDIN (the runtime-delivered flag, per code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks) and exit 0 / allow when true. The run_state.stop_hook_active column (E7-REQ11.1 seq 189, populated by NEW-02 seq 258) is a DURABLE MIRROR for the loop driver, NOT the gate input — the stdin flag is authoritative. Add a unit test feeding {stop_hook_active:true,...} on stdin and asserting allow()/exit 0 regardless of unproven in-scope items.' Then add a relates_to edge seq 236 <-> seq 189 documenting the stdin-vs-DB split (POST .../work-items/{id}/relations/, since the applier never created edges per GT-D2).

verified.title: All three hooks print the steering reason as JSON to STDOUT then exit 2 (runtime reads only STDERR on exit 2, so every block silently drops its reason); NO board item owns the channel fix. Prior pass cited wrong seqs (615/259); real items are seq 214 + seq 124
verified.note: CONFIRMED in substance but the prior finding's seq citations are WRONG and must be corrected before any edit lands on the wrong item. On disk: pre_tool_use_hook.py main() does print(json.dumps(decision)) (L75) then 'return 0 if allow else 2' (L76); stop_hook.py main() prints decision to stdout (L214) then exit 2 (L215); subagent_stop_hook.py prints json (L109) then exit 2 (L110). All three write the reason to STDOUT; runtime discards stdout on exit 2 (only stderr fed back). The two board ACs asserting stderr behavior the code never produces: seq 214 (task:ASCP-E8-13#3, 'Property 21 PBT: exit 0 proceeds, exit 2 blocks with stderr fed back') and seq 124 (task:ASCP-E4-S1#5, 'Assert stderr names the blocking item ID'). The finding cited 'seq 615' and 'seq 259' — those are DIGEST LINE numbers, not seq ids; seq 259 is actually NEW-03 and seq 615 does not exist. Digest grep for stderr returns only these two; no 'permissionDecision'/'hookSpecificOutput'/stdout-fix item exists, so 'no item owns the channel fix' HOLDS. Revised only to repair board_items; severity P1 upheld (steering channel dead on every block). Evidence: pre_tool_use_hook.py:75-76; stop_hook.py:214-215; subagent_stop_hook.py:109-110; live seq 214/124 desc.

recommended_action.detail: Create a NEW owning item (e.g. NEW-15, Module E8, Phase 0, type:wiring, gate:stop): 'Fix hook output channel: emit steering reason on the runtime-honored channel'. AC: on a block, write the located reason to STDERR and exit 2 (PreToolUse/Stop/SubagentStop), OR switch to exit-0 conforming JSON (PreToolUse: hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision=deny + permissionDecisionReason + hookEventName; Stop/SubagentStop: top-level decision:block + reason). Stop printing the decision dict to stdout on the exit-2 path (pre_tool_use_hook.py L75, stop_hook.py L214, subagent_stop_hook.py L109). Add blocked_by edges: seq 214 (Property 21) blocked_by NEW-15 and seq 124 (E4-S1.6) blocked_by NEW-15. Cite code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks exit-code semantics + best-practices G4. CORRECT the prior seq citations: Property-21 PBT = seq 214 (NOT 615); E4-S1.6 = seq 124 (NOT 259).

verified.title: subagent_stop_hook.py returns decision:'approve' (not a valid SubagentStop value); the rename is only a buried note on the E8-21 STORY (seq 61), no task owns the code edit. Prior pass mis-cited the note location (seq 234) and the omission_guard owner (seq 247)

Evidence: subagent_stop_hook.py:48,84,110; best-practices 03 L141; live story seq 61 desc; digest seq 248.

(b) apply_log shows df8b5217 PATCHed TWICE; the second op's text won. (c) Live GET: story:REQ-INFRA-005 (seq 20, Spec-Verified) description = 'Reframe AC#4 to depend on NEW-12 authoring the migrations' — NEW-12's own AC1-4 (author 8 migrations / run_state completeness / gate_audit_log append-only grant) is gone. (d) Live by_external_id has NEW-01..11,13,14 but NO ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-12; audit roots = 13. (e) plan _meta.counts.new=14 vs apply_log 13 creates; report line 56 says 'creates 14 NEW owning items'. Claim!=reality holds, but this restates GT-D1 with extra mechanism, so verdict=duplicate; action preserved as the most actionable remediation.

Must be created with match_key=null AND a guard in apply_upsert_plan.py so op['op']=='create' takes the create branch even when a match_key resolves — the current resolve() ignores op['op'], which is the root cause of the collision.

Report line 370 claims '5 priority:blocking items (NEW-01/02/05/08/12 + E8-13)'; NEW-12 doesn't exist and the rest lack the label. Refinement: E8-13 DOES have native priority=urgent, but the report's view groups by the priority:* LABEL which is what's missing, so the label-grouped triage is empty for the spine — claim!=reality holds.

Patch apply_upsert_plan.py to fold op['priority'] into the label_ids set (and/or set native priority) so re-runs are idempotent.

verified.title: NEW-09 created as a live duplicate execution_bounds owner alongside UPD-E8-20 (preferred fold) — both landed
verified.note: CONFIRMED-BUT-REVISED to P2. Real: plan NEW-09 conditional='only if UPD-E8-20 not applied'; UPD-E8-20 supersedes='NEW-09'; apply_upsert_plan.py has zero conditional/supersedes handling (executes every op, loop line 184). Live board: E8-20 (seq 60, Spec-Verified) desc='Expand E8-20 to OWN authoring tools/execution_bounds (PREFERRED over a standalone item)'; NEW-09 (seq 256, Backlog) ALSO exists, desc='Conditional create. Only instantiate if UPD-E8-20 is NOT taken... executor should prefer the E8-20 fold and skip this create'. Both name tools/execution_bounds. WHY P2 not P1: NEW-09 is in Backlog (not Spec-Verified) and self-deprecates / points to E8-20 as preferred, so an executor reading it is steered to skip it — a redundancy/ambiguity that pollutes a 'who owns execution_bounds' query, not an active competing owner that misdirects execution. Severity lowered to match blast-radius.

recommended_action.detail: Close/cancel ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-09 (low blast-radius, already Backlog) with a note pointing to ASCP-E8-20 as the live owner, OR set NEW-09 relates_to E8-20 and re-label as superseded placeholder. Long-term: teach the applier to honor conditional/supersedes so guard ops are skipped.

verified.title: .apply_log.json is a plan-echo with no per-op outcome — no result/http-status/error, which is why every silent drop went uncaught
verified.note: CONFIRMED. apply_upsert_plan.py log entries (lines 202, 217) are {op,name,id|external_id,fields} only; fields=list(body)=keys SENT, not accepted. Live inspection of .apply_log.json: 39 actions, key-sets exactly {('fields','id','name','op'),('external_id','fields','name','op')} — NO status/result/error/http_status/returned_id/collided_with key anywhere. df8b5217 appears as TWO update actions sitting silently adjacent with no collision signal. Log records 13 creates + 26 updates while plan _meta.counts says new:14/update:25 — that count mismatch is the only (passive) trace of the NEW-12 demotion, and the log cannot surface the dropped relations, dropped priority, or 6 untouched E7 stories. 'It's in the apply log' is not evidence the effect landed; the live board is the only valid oracle. Claim (faithful applied record) != reality (write-intent echo).

recommended_action.detail: Tooling fix: extend apply_upsert_plan.py to capture per-op {http_status, returned_id, error, collided_with} and assert each op['op']=='create' yielded a NEW external_id (not a resolve-to-existing). Add a post-apply reconcile step that re-GETs each touched item and diffs sent-vs-landed desc/state/labels. Until then treat the live board, not the log, as the apply-integrity oracle.
