verifiers.dimension: Adversarial verification of token-waste-loop findings (TWL-01..08) against the live Plane board and on-disk reality

verified.title: CH-10 escalate-don't-re-inject rung never implemented; the 49x loop fix is unactioned finding-text inside E5-S10's live description
verified.note: Independently confirmed against live board + on-disk. E5-S10 (seq 37, id 76e1a1a6, updated 2026-06-18T17:39) live description_html ends VERBATIM: 'ALSO omits the CH-10 escalate-don't-re-inject rung that directly closes C-LOOP-04 — add it.' That is unactioned finding-text, not an AC. Live descriptions of all four E5-S10 subtasks (142/143/144/145) and all five E8-21 subtasks (234-238): 234=HANDOFF-before-block ordering, 235=lex-specialis tiebreak, 236=reentrancy composition, 237=Z3 fixtures, 238=Plane mirror — NONE implements block_streak / _escalate / a rung ladder. On-disk .claude/hooks/stop_hook.py (full, 219 lines) has NO _escalate, NO _block_streak, NO block_streak read; the unproven gate (lines 174-179) emits a bare 'Stop blocked: N in-scope item(s) not proven: [ids]' with no escalation. Report line 102/122 marks C-LOOP-04 RESOLVED; lines 179/336 say 'add the CH-10 rung'. CLAIM(resolved)!=REALITY(unauthored) holds NOW. Correction to prior evidence: 02-stop_hook §1 carries a _block_streak FALLBACK (lines 79-89) that varies the string even with no D3 populator, so the proposed mechanism is NOT 'the only thing' preventing re-injection and does not depend on NEW-02 — this strengthens the finding: fully specced yet zero board ACs implement it. P0 stands.

recommended_action.detail: UPDATE story:ASCP-E5-S10 (seq 37): replace the trailing finding-text with a concrete AC, AND add/repurpose a subtask under the single owner (E8-21, per TWL-04) implementing the CH-10 ladder per 02-stop_hook §1/§2.3: rung-0 locate+act, rung-1 acknowledge-repeat+redirect (single _escalate template), rung>=BLOCK_ESCALATION_CAP=3 -> HANDOFF (exit 0). Require a unit test asserting a repeated identical block escalates (string differs at rung-1) then converts to HANDOFF, never re-injects byte-for-byte. Since 02-stop_hook ships a hook-owned fallback streak, the test must NOT depend on NEW-02 being wired.

verified.title: Reentrancy guard specced on the wrong signal (run_state.stop_hook_active self-mutex) — will not suppress Claude Code's forced-continuation re-injection
verified.note: Confirmed against live ACs and on-disk. E5-S10.1 (seq 142, live desc): 'Implement with_reentrancy_guard(fn): set stop_hook_active=True; skip if already set; release only on allow.' E8-21.3 (seq 236, live desc): 'with_reentrancy_guard(evaluate_stop) via run_state.stop_hook_active; release the flag only when the blocking condition clears. Unit test asserts no cascade during a blocking cycle.' Both describe a hook-WRITTEN self-mutex keyed on run_state. The canonical mechanism (02-stop_hook §1 is_reentrant lines 47-56 + order-of-ops 292-296; charter CH-07 line 160) READS event.get('stop_hook_active') from the Stop event stdin and returns 0 FIRST. On-disk stop_hook.py main() (lines 201-215) never reads stop_hook_active (net-new). Claude Code writes the event payload, not run_state, so a run_state-keyed mutex 'released only on allow' cannot detect re-entry (and would in fact stay set across a blocking cycle — opposite of allow-on-reentry). E8-21.3's only test ('no cascade during a blocking cycle') tests the self-mutex, not event-level zero-token re-entry. Report success criterion #4 (line 442) 'A Stop re-entry emits zero tokens' is correct but no board AC asserts it. CLAIM!=REALITY holds.

recommended_action.detail: UPDATE task:ASCP-E8-21#2 (seq 236) and task:ASCP-E5-S10#0 (seq 142): the guard MUST read event['stop_hook_active'] from the Stop event payload as the FIRST action in main() and return exit 0 with zero stdout/stderr (02-stop_hook §1 is_reentrant + order-of-operations). Remove the run_state.stop_hook_active set/'release-only-on-allow' self-mutex framing. Add AC + unit test: a Stop event carrying stop_hook_active:true produces exit 0 and emits zero steering tokens (report success criterion #4).

verified.title: NEW-02 (D3 populator) has zero relations and zero ACs on the live board — the HANDOFF/idle fix depends on it but the dependency and its load-bearing test exist only in prose
verified.note: Confirmed live. GET /work-items/{NEW-02 id 80d42539}/relations/ returns EMPTY-ALL (every bucket empty); E8-21, E8-14, E5-S10, E8-13, E5-S10.1, E8-21.3 ALL return EMPTY-ALL too — consistent with GT-D2 (apply_upsert_plan.py has no relation-create capability). NEW-02 live description (411 chars) is rationale prose only ('This story owns the writer. Lands LAST in Phase-0...') — it does NOT carry report §5 AC1 (counter-list write), AC2 (BLOCKED-ON sentinel parser), or AC3 ('a test proves a 3-streak triggers HANDOFF, not re-injection', report line 295). NEW-03/10/13 live descriptions are also prose-only (hasAC=False). Report line 123 marks C-IDLE-03 'RESOLVED (behavior live after D3)' and line 419 calls NEW-02 the milestone 'that turns anti-loopmaxxing from inert to firing', yet it is an unactioned stub with no consumer edges, so apply-order can schedule consumers before the populator. CLAIM!=REALITY holds. SIBLING of GT-D1 (NEW item created as stub) and GT-D2 (relations never created), not a restatement — the specific NEW-02 instance with AC3's test missing.

recommended_action.detail: UPDATE ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-02 (seq 258) to carry report §5 AC1-AC3 verbatim, especially AC3 (a 3-streak -> HANDOFF, not re-injection) and AC2 (BLOCKED-ON: <dependency> -> run_state.external_blocker). THEN add blocked_by edges via the LIVE relations endpoint (NOT apply_upsert_plan.py — GT-D2 class) from E8-14, E8-21, story:ASCP-E5-S10, ASCP-E3-4, ASCP-E4-S1, E6-S2/S3/S5 to NEW-02 per report line 153. Caveat in the AC that 02-stop_hook's hook-owned fallback streak makes the ladder non-degenerate-until-wired, so NEW-02 unblocks the EXACT signal, not the only signal.

verified.title: Reentrancy + escalation-rung ownership is triply-claimed and self-contradictory across E5-S10, E8-14, E8-21 on the live board
verified.note: Confirmed live, with one sharpening. Report R5 (line 197) DOES state a clean contract: E8-14 owns the reentrancy guard, E8-21 owns the escalation/HANDOFF rungs. E8-21 story (seq 61) live desc echoes it: 'E8-21 owns the escalation/HANDOFF rungs inside evaluate_stop; E8-14 owns the reentrancy guard half.' BUT the board CONTRADICTS that contract three ways: (1) E8-21.3 (seq 236) is titled 'Implement the reentrancy guard composition' and its AC implements with_reentrancy_guard — placing reentrancy under E8-21, not E8-14; (2) E5-S10.1 (seq 142) ALSO implements with_reentrancy_guard — a third claimant; (3) E8-14 story (seq 56) live desc says 'E8-14 owns ONLY the 005_run_state.sql column migration — it does NOT populate the counters' and never states it owns the reentrancy guard — directly contradicting R5's assignment. So three subtasks/stories touch the guard and NONE owns the escalation rung as a subtask (TWL-01). R6 'cite-don't-restate' is sound for the PRINCIPLE but the block_streak ladder is hook CODE (02-stop_hook §1), not citable A8/charter prose, so citing would leave the mechanism unimplemented. CLAIM(clean contract)!=REALITY(board encodes the opposite) holds. P1 retained (misleads an executor; TWL-01/02 carry the P0 break).

recommended_action.detail: UPDATE the three items to a single non-overlapping contract matching R5: assign the EVENT-LEVEL reentrancy guard to exactly ONE item (E8-14 seq 56, adding an explicit reentrancy AC since its desc currently disclaims everything but the migration), retitle/repoint E8-21.3 (seq 236) to the block_streak escalation LADDER (not reentrancy), and rewrite E5-S10.1 (seq 142) to CONSUME both rather than re-implement with_reentrancy_guard. Clarify R6 on E8-21 (seq 61): charter/A8 own the PRINCIPLE (CH-10) + BLOCKED-ON sentinel, but the rung MECHANISM (block_streak ladder + _escalate template) is hook code that must be implemented, not merely cited.

verified.title: Report NEW-13 AC2 mislabels escalate-don't-re-inject as CH-08 (clause numbering broadly skewed); canonical clause is CH-10 — but the mislabel lives in the report doc, not yet on the live board
verified.note: Real but mis-scoped and over-severe. CONFIRMED at the report layer: report line 336 AC2 reads 'CH-08 (escalate-don't-re-inject)' while charter 00-charter.md line 217 defines CH-10='Vary or escalate on repeat; bounded 2-3 rungs -> HANDOFF' and line 178 defines CH-08='HANDOFF before block, as a pure data predicate.' Same AC2 also mis-maps CH-07 (it labels CH-07='located oracle before any done-claim', whereas charter line 160 defines CH-07='Reentrancy/read stop_hook_active') — the AC2 numbering is broadly skewed, not just one clause. SKEPTIC correction: the LIVE NEW-13 board item (seq 260, id 2f6d9abb) does NOT carry any CH-08/CH-10 numeric label — its live description uses prose 'escalate-don't-re-inject' with no clause number (hasAC=False). So claim!=reality does NOT yet hold ON THE LIVE BOARD; the defect is a latent report-doc error that would mis-encode the board only if AC2 is copied verbatim. Downgraded P1->P2 (doc-latent, not a live contradiction today) and scoped to the report + the act of authoring NEW-13's AC.

recommended_action.detail: FIX report NEW-13 AC2 (line 336) clause numbering before it lands: escalate-don't-re-inject=CH-10 (charter line 217); HANDOFF-before-block=CH-08 (line 178); reentrancy=CH-07 (line 160). When authoring NEW-13 (seq 260) acceptance text, encode escalate-don't-re-inject as CH-10. Ensure E5-S10/E8-21 cite CH-10 for the rung and CH-07 for reentrancy.

verified.title: No board item owns the phantom-field→content-diff fix; live PreToolUse status/in_scope authority is inert on every real Edit/Write (and human_signed is read from the payload too)
verified.note: INDEPENDENTLY CONFIRMED against live disk + live board. (1) Live .claude/hooks/pre_tool_use_hook.py:37 `field = (tool_input or {}).get("field")`; lines 41/44 gate R1/R2 on `field == "status"` / `field == "in_scope"`. A real Edit payload is {file_path,old_string,new_string} and Write is {file_path,content} — neither carries `field`, so the entire status/in_scope authority branch is dead pre-write. (2) Whole-board scan of all 256 snapshot desc_text for phantom|content-diff|_changed_coverage|old_string|new_string|JSON delta returned NONE — no item carries an AC to replace tool_input['field'] with a diff. (3) Live E4-S2 (seq34) and E4-S3 (seq35) descs say only 'EXTENDS the unified hook (NEW-10)' = the PostToolUse five-layer leg; NEW-10 (seq253) desc confirms it 'builds the one [PostToolUse] hook both stories extend' — not the PreToolUse authority predicate. Artifact 03-pre_tool_use-steering.md §2 (`_changed_coverage_fields`) + §3 (the four AP-14 regression tests) is the load-bearing fix and is unowned. SIBLING DEFECT of the same class, confirmed in the same file: live line 74 `human_signed=bool(ti.get("human_signed", False))` reads the in_scope authority signal FROM tool_input — contradicting the hook's own docstring (line 13 'never from the payload') and artifact 03 fix #1; an agent can set human_signed=true in its own Write payload and self-exempt an in_scope flip even after the field predicate is fixed. Both are the same generalization gap.

recommended_action.detail: Create a Phase-0 blocking story 'Generalize pre_tool_use_hook.py authority gate (artifact 03): phantom-field→content-diff + out-of-band human_signed'. ACs: (1) replace the tool_input['field'] branch with _changed_coverage_fields diffing old_string/new_string (Edit) and on-disk-content-vs-content (Write); (2) COVERAGE_MODEL_PATHS + PROTECTED_PREFIXES read from tools/execution_bounds with current literals as defaults; (3) human_signed resolved out-of-band via _resolve_human_signed (HUMAN_SIGNED env / .claude/approvals/{session_id}.human-signed), NEVER from tool_input — fixes the live line-74 self-exempt hole; (4) pin the R1-R5 verbatim catalog + the four AP-14 + two self-exemption regression tests from artifact 03 §3. resolves:['G6','AP-14','A-INTENT-01','artifact:03']. Block it by E8-13/E8-13#0 (registration) and NEW-05 (verifier.md literal). Closest existing home if update-over-create preferred: task:ASCP-E4-S3#3 or a new E4-S1 subtask.

verified.title: COH-1 fail-closed-but-stuck contradiction is UNFIXED on the live board: subtask seq 131 still specifies suffix-less `name: verifier`
verified.note: INDEPENDENTLY CONFIRMED via live API. GET work-items/{E4-S3#0=55e5291b}/ live description verbatim: 'Write frontmatter (name: verifier, model: claude-opus-4-8, tools: [Read, Grep, Glob, Bash])'. The hooks pin the literal 'verifier.md' (pre_tool_use_hook.py:25 VERIFIER_AGENT='verifier.md'; subagent_stop_hook.py:30 same) and actor_identity resolves actor from CLAUDE_AGENT_NAME (GT-7). A suffix-less verifier resolves to actor 'verifier' != 'verifier.md', blocking every proven-flip (fail-closed-but-stuck). NEW-05 (seq254) live desc records 'DECISION (apply-step-0): take option (a) name: verifier.md' and E8-20 (seq60) live desc adds 'VERIFIER_ROLE default MUST be byte-consistent with the COH-1 reconciliation (verifier.md)' — so two/three live items now give OPPOSITE frontmatter instructions, and the brittle one (seq131) is the buildable subtask an implementer follows verbatim. Prior pass created the right-convention owning item but never edited the contradicting subtask.

recommended_action.detail: Update task:ASCP-E4-S3#0 (seq131): change 'name: verifier' to 'name: verifier.md' and append 'COH-1: the frontmatter name MUST be byte-identical to the hooks VERIFIER_AGENT literal verifier.md; a suffix-less name fail-closes every proven-flip (GT-7).' verified.title: SubagentStop role-separation subtask seq 134 still trusts payload actor_agent and emits bare-verdict exit-2 strings, contradicting the live hook and artifact 04; the SubagentStop channel/string rewrite is unowned
verified.note: CONFIRMED with one scope nuance. GET work-items/{E4-S3#3=065102c3}/ live desc verbatim: 'Parse the Subagent-Result Envelope from stdin (actor_agent, requirement_id, omission_declaration, optional evidence[]) ... role-separation block (actor_agent == implementer.md -> exit 2) ... exit 2 naming missing fields.' This reads+trusts actor_agent FROM the envelope and emits bare exit-2 reasons. Live subagent_stop_hook.py:98 already resolves actor via resolve_identity(CLAUDE_AGENT_NAME) and:60 rejects a record actor_agent that mismatches the runtime actor (forgery) — so the buildable subtask directly contradicts the shipped hook and artifact 04 fix #1. Live hook also prints JSON to STDOUT on exit 2 (line 109) = the G4 channel bug (exit-2 reads stderr only), has TEN bare-verdict reason strings (lines 53/59/62/69/72/76/78/82), and no stop_hook_active guard (G2). SCOPE NUANCE (the original finding slightly over-stated 'no item owns COH-3'): E8-21 (seq61) live desc DOES own the COH-3 approve->allow lockstep-test leg and the escalation rungs ('the COH-3 accept-value note (A4 approve->allow; update lockstep test)'). But board scan confirms NO item owns the SubagentStop bare-verdict→located+action string-catalog rewrite, the G4 stderr channel fix, or the G2 stop_hook_active guard — the stderr/stop_hook_active board hits (seq214, seq236) are all for the STOP hook, not SubagentStop. The load-bearing claim (seq134 contradiction + unowned channel/string-catalog/G2) is fully intact.

recommended_action.detail: Update task:ASCP-E4-S3#3 (seq134): replace 'Parse the Subagent-Result Envelope from stdin (actor_agent...) then role-separation block (actor_agent == implementer.md -> exit 2)' with 'Resolve actor via tools/actor_identity (CLAUDE_AGENT_NAME); block when resolved_actor != verifier.md OR record.actor_agent != resolved_actor (artifact 04 fix #1). Emit located+action reasons to STDERR on exit 2 (the live hook wrongly prints JSON to stdout); honor stop_hook_active reentrancy.' Separately widen UPD-E8-21 (seq61) ACs to OWN the full artifact-04 SubagentStop string-catalog rewrite + the G4 stderr channel fix + the G2 stop_hook_active guard (it already owns COH-3 approve->allow + escalation), giving the SubagentStop leg a single owner.

verified.title: Every claimed dependency/ordering edge for the dynamism chain is absent as a live relation (GT-D2 generalized across the graph)
verified.note: INDEPENDENTLY CONFIRMED via live GET /work-items/{id}/relations/. All buckets EMPTY (blocking/blocked_by/relates_to/duplicate = []) on every item queried before/through rate-limiting: E8-13, E8-20, E8-21, E8-14, E2-S2, E4-S2, E4-S3, E4-S1, NEW-01, E8-13#0 (10 distinct items, ALL EMPTY). Yet NEW-14 (seq251) live desc CLAIMS 'This story adds the explicit Plane blocked-by relations so the Active Sprint/Roadmap ordering cannot schedule a hook-consuming story ahead of the registration', and reorg notes describe edges (E2-S2 'hard dependency on NEW-05', E8-21 'Add the NEW-02 dependency', E8-14 'Cross-link NEW-02', NEW-02 'lands LAST'). kept.title: Reentrancy guard (seq 236) reads stop_hook_active from the run_state DB row, but the runtime delivers it on Stop-event STDIN; stop_hook.py never reads it and with_reentrancy_guard is unimplemented
kept.note: CONFIRMED on live board + on disk. Live work-item 7b259aa5 (seq 236, task:ASCP-E8-21#2) NAME='ASCP-E8-21.3 Implement the reentrancy guard composition', live desc='with_reentrancy_guard(evaluate_stop) via run_state.stop_hook_active; release the flag only when the blocking condition clears...'. On disk: grep for 'with_reentrancy_guard' and 'stop_hook_active' across .claude/hooks/ and tools/ returns ZERO matches (exit 1) — the guard is unimplemented. stop_hook.py main() reads ONLY event.get('run_state') (L211) and event.get('feature_list') (L212) from stdin; never reads the top-level stop_hook_active boolean the runtime delivers per code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks. Seq 189 (E7-REQ11.1) and seq 217 (E8-14.3) add stop_hook_active as a run_state DB column populated by NEW-02's loop driver (seq 258), not by the runtime. Live relations on seq 236 AND seq 189 are ALL EMPTY (blocked_by/blocking/relates_to=0), so the recommended relates_to edge does not yet exist (consistent with GT-D2). Keying the anti-loop guard off a DB column the runtime never sets means a persistently-unproven item still blocks to the runtime block-cap and is force-overridden (#63674). Claim (DB-column gate) != reality (stdin flag authoritative). P1 upheld. Evidence: live seq 236 desc; stop_hook.py:211-212; grep zero hits; digest seq 189/520; empty relations on 236 & 189.

recommended_action.detail: Amend seq 236 (task:ASCP-E8-21#2) AC: 'The reentrancy guard MUST read the top-level stop_hook_active boolean from the Stop/SubagentStop event STDIN (the runtime-delivered flag, per code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks) and exit 0 / allow when true. The run_state.stop_hook_active column (E7-REQ11.1 seq 189, populated by NEW-02 seq 258) is a DURABLE MIRROR for the loop driver, NOT the gate input — the stdin flag is authoritative. Add a unit test feeding {stop_hook_active:true,...} on stdin and asserting allow()/exit 0 regardless of unproven in-scope items.' Then add a relates_to edge seq 236 <-> seq 189 documenting the stdin-vs-DB split (POST .../work-items/{id}/relations/, since the applier never created edges per GT-D2).

kept.title: All three hooks print the steering reason as JSON to STDOUT then exit 2 (runtime reads only STDERR on exit 2, so every block silently drops its reason); NO board item owns the channel fix. Prior pass cited wrong seqs (615/259); real items are seq 214 + seq 124
kept.note: CONFIRMED in substance but the prior finding's seq citations are WRONG and must be corrected before any edit lands on the wrong item. On disk: pre_tool_use_hook.py main() does print(json.dumps(decision)) (L75) then 'return 0 if allow else 2' (L76); stop_hook.py main() prints decision to stdout (L214) then exit 2 (L215); subagent_stop_hook.py prints json (L109) then exit 2 (L110). All three write the reason to STDOUT; runtime discards stdout on exit 2 (only stderr fed back). The two board ACs asserting stderr behavior the code never produces: seq 214 (task:ASCP-E8-13#3, 'Property 21 PBT: exit 0 proceeds, exit 2 blocks with stderr fed back') and seq 124 (task:ASCP-E4-S1#5, 'Assert stderr names the blocking item ID'). The finding cited 'seq 615' and 'seq 259' — those are DIGEST LINE numbers, not seq ids; seq 259 is actually NEW-03 and seq 615 does not exist. Digest grep for stderr returns only these two; no 'permissionDecision'/'hookSpecificOutput'/stdout-fix item exists, so 'no item owns the channel fix' HOLDS. Revised only to repair board_items; severity P1 upheld (steering channel dead on every block). Evidence: pre_tool_use_hook.py:75-76; stop_hook.py:214-215; subagent_stop_hook.py:109-110; live seq 214/124 desc.

recommended_action.detail: Create a NEW owning item (e.g. NEW-15, Module E8, Phase 0, type:wiring, gate:stop): 'Fix hook output channel: emit steering reason on the runtime-honored channel'. AC: on a block, write the located reason to STDERR and exit 2 (PreToolUse/Stop/SubagentStop), OR switch to exit-0 conforming JSON (PreToolUse: hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision=deny + permissionDecisionReason + hookEventName; Stop/SubagentStop: top-level decision:block + reason). Stop printing the decision dict to stdout on the exit-2 path (pre_tool_use_hook.py L75, stop_hook.py L214, subagent_stop_hook.py L109). Add blocked_by edges: seq 214 (Property 21) blocked_by NEW-15 and seq 124 (E4-S1.6) blocked_by NEW-15. Cite code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks exit-code semantics + best-practices G4. CORRECT the prior seq citations: Property-21 PBT = seq 214 (NOT 615); E4-S1.6 = seq 124 (NOT 259).

The COH-3 'A4 approve->allow' note lives on the E8-21 STORY (seq 61, live desc '...Add ... the COH-3 accept-value note (A4 approve->allow; update lockstep test)'), NOT on seq 234 (which is E8-21.1 HANDOFF ordering) as the finding claimed. kept.title: Reentrancy guard specced on the wrong signal (run_state.stop_hook_active self-mutex) — will not suppress Claude Code's forced-continuation re-injection
kept.note: Confirmed against live ACs and on-disk. E5-S10.1 (seq 142, live desc): 'Implement with_reentrancy_guard(fn): set stop_hook_active=True; skip if already set; release only on allow.' E8-21.3 (seq 236, live desc): 'with_reentrancy_guard(evaluate_stop) via run_state.stop_hook_active; release the flag only when the blocking condition clears. Unit test asserts no cascade during a blocking cycle.' Both describe a hook-WRITTEN self-mutex keyed on run_state. The canonical mechanism (02-stop_hook §1 is_reentrant lines 47-56 + order-of-ops 292-296; charter CH-07 line 160) READS event.get('stop_hook_active') from the Stop event stdin and returns 0 FIRST. On-disk stop_hook.py main() (lines 201-215) never reads stop_hook_active (net-new). Claude Code writes the event payload, not run_state, so a run_state-keyed mutex 'released only on allow' cannot detect re-entry (and would in fact stay set across a blocking cycle — opposite of allow-on-reentry). E8-21.3's only test ('no cascade during a blocking cycle') tests the self-mutex, not event-level zero-token re-entry. Report success criterion #4 (line 442) 'A Stop re-entry emits zero tokens' is correct but no board AC asserts it. CLAIM!=REALITY holds.

kept.note: CONFIRMED. CODE: apply_upsert_plan.py builds the PATCH/CREATE body (lines 187-217) from only description_html/state/labels; op['acceptance_criteria'] is never serialized. LOG: every action's fields=[description_html,state,labels]. PLAN: 39/39 ops carry a non-empty acceptance_criteria[] (python-verified). LIVE API (read today): E8-13, NEW-05, NEW-02, NEW-08, NEW-01 descriptions contain ZERO 'ACn' markers — only the 3-5-sentence prose note (e.g. E8-13 live desc len=468, ends after 'agent_role=human retained'; AC1 'exactly five governance events', AC5 ralph Stop-ownership telemetry, AC6 SPINE_REQUIRED_EVENTS are absent). ONE correction to the prior framing: NEW-14's live desc DOES contain 'ACn' substrings, but those are incidental cross-references ('E4-S1 AC4/AC5 describe pre_tool_use...'), not NEW-14's own structured ACs — so the '10 of 13 NEW items lack AC markers' count is imprecise, but the load-bearing claim (the applier writes no acceptance_criteria; the testable contracts the report's coverage matrices treat as landed exist only in the JSON) is fully confirmed. P0 stands.

recommended_action.detail: Extend the applier to append an '## Acceptance Criteria' block (rendered from op['acceptance_criteria']) into description_html on create/update, then re-PATCH each item. Highest priority: ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-05 (COH-1 byte-consistency AC3), NEW-02 (D3 run_state counter list AC1, BLOCKED-ON sentinel AC2/AC4), NEW-08 (validate_spec four-field verdict AC1), and story:ASCP-E8-13 (AC1 five-events, AC5 ralph telemetry, AC6 SPINE_REQUIRED_EVENTS) — the spine-critical testable contracts currently invisible on the board.

kept.title: GT-D1 root cause pinned and blast radius widened: NEW-12 op carries a match_key so it could never create; dangling-referenced by 4 live items
kept.note: CONFIRMED, and materially additive to GT-D1 (not a mere restatement). CODE+PLAN: op id=NEW-12 has op='create' BUT match_key='story:REQ-INFRA-005'; apply_upsert_plan.py:195 calls resolve(op) BEFORE the create branch, and resolve() matches match_key first (lines 139-142) -> returns the existing REQ-INFRA-005 id, so the 'create' executed as an UPDATE of df8b5217. LOG confirms TWO update actions on df8b5217 (NEW-12 op then UPD-REQ-INFRA-005 op) — the second clobbered the first. LIVE API (read today): external_id ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-12 returns NOT FOUND; no item by that name exists; only 13 audit items exist though plan _meta.counts.new=14. The NEW-12 reference is dangling in REQ-INFRA-005 live desc ('depend on NEW-12 authoring the migrations') and in E3-1/E3-2 precondition blocks ('after NEW-08 + NEW-12 + spine'); E3-1/E3-2 are thus gated on an owning item that can never be reached. This pins the GT-D1 root cause to a specific bug (match_key on a create op + resolve-before-create ordering) and widens the dangling scope from 1 item (GT-D1) to 4. P1 appropriate.

recommended_action.detail: Create standalone NEW-12 (E1, Phase 0, priority:blocking, external_id ascp-audit-reorg:NEW-12, state Spec-Verified) owning 'author eight .kiro migrations 001-008 + run_state completeness columns + gate_audit_log append-only grant' with plan AC1-AC4. Root-cause code fix: strip match_key from any op whose op=='create', OR make the applier treat op=='create' as create-only regardless of match_key (skip the resolve() shortcut for creates). After creation the NEW-12 references in REQ-INFRA-005/E3-1/E3-2/E7-REQ16 resolve.

kept.title: E8-13 parent/child PreCompact contradiction: parent wires 5 events (no PreCompact) while child task #0 still says register 6 incl. PreCompact + smoke-test all hooks command-type
kept.note: CONFIRMED on live + snapshot. Parent story (seq 55, story:ASCP-E8-13) live desc: 'Wires SessionStart / PreToolUse / PostToolUse / SubagentStop / Stop' = 5 events, PreCompact omitted (the C1 rewrite). Child task #0 (seq 211, task:ASCP-E8-13#0, untouched by the prior pass — no apply-log entry) reads: 'Register Stop, PreToolUse, PostToolUse (matcher Write|Edit|MultiEdit), SubagentStop, SessionStart, PreCompact as type:command (task 9.1)... Write tests/smoke/test_hook_config.py asserting all hooks are command-type and the four subagents are discoverable.' Remediation 05 lines 120/356/434 mark PreCompact (D7) deliberately NOT registered / NOT AUTHORED; GT-3 confirms no pre_compact_hook.py on disk; GT-2 confirms .claude/agents/ is .gitkeep-only so 'four subagents discoverable' also fails. Parent and child now disagree on event count (5 vs 6) and the child's smoke test would assert a 6th hook that the parent's design omits. Genuinely new (not GT-D1/2/3). P1 stands.

recommended_action.detail: Edit task:ASCP-E8-13#0 (seq 211) to register exactly five events (drop PreCompact), align the smoke test to assert only the five registered hooks (and gate the 'four subagents discoverable' assertion on NEW-05 landing the agent frontmatter, since .claude/agents/ is currently .gitkeep-only). Add a relates_to edge from task:ASCP-E8-13#0 to NEW-11 (PreCompact/D7) so the deferred 6th event is tracked, not silently dropped.

kept.title: Phase-0 spec-completion items sit Spec-Verified while their load-bearing prereqs are NOT AUTHORED — softer outrun-state inconsistency (one cited item mis-stated)
kept.note: REVISED: real but the evidence was wrong on one item. LIVE API (read today): E5-S10=Spec-Verified, E8-21=Spec-Verified, NEW-02=Spec-Verified — all three CONSUME or ARE the D3 run_state populator which remediation 05 §7.1 line 432 classes NOT AUTHORED (and GT-6 confirms no such tool on disk), so the inconsistency with the C16/Kanban 'state reflects on-disk reality' rule is real. BUT the finding also cited ASCP-E3-4 as Spec-Verified; live state is Spec-Compiling (already off the verified tier), so E3-4 is dropped from board_items and the claim is narrowed. This remains a defensible posture (Spec-Verified = the SPEC is verified, not the implementation), hence op:none / flag-for-awareness rather than a forced true-up; the inconsistency is that the prior pass applied the C16 'state must reflect on-disk reality' rule to E7 but not to these Phase-0 consumers or to NEW-02 itself. P2 (polish/consistency), not P1.

recommended_action.detail: Record one decision and apply it uniformly: EITHER keep E5-S10/E8-21/NEW-02 at Spec-Verified and add a one-line note that Spec-Verified means spec-only (implementation tracked separately), OR true-up NEW-02 (and the consumers' state claims) until the D3 populator/NEW-08 validator are authored — matching how C16 was enforced on E7. Do not leave the rule applied to E7 but not here.

kept.title: acceptance_criteria is a phantom field: applier drops it for ALL 39 ops, so the R1/R5/R6/COH-1 contracts (NEW-10 AC2, E8-21 AC2/AC3, NEW-03 AC6, E8-20 AC3) never landed; R5 scope contract is live-prose-only with zero relations
kept.note:
VERDICT revised: core CONFIRMED, sub-claim mis-scoped. INDEPENDENTLY CONFIRMED against live board + plan on-disk:
(1) PHANTOM FIELD: plane-upsert-plan.json carries acceptance_criteria on 39/39 ops (union-of-keys verified). apply_upsert_plan.py lines 187-194 build body from ONLY description_html/state/labels; acceptance_criteria is never read, never rendered into description_html. .apply_log.json fields == [description_html,state,labels] for all ops. So every AC was silently dropped. This is a NEW mechanism, not a restatement of GT-D2/D3 (those are relations + apply-collision); this is a distinct data-loss path.
(2) NAMED LOST CONTRACTS confirmed in the plan's acceptance_criteria but ABSENT from live description_html: NEW-10 AC2 (R1 'E4-S2/E4-S3 EXTEND this hook; neither re-registers'); E8-21 AC2 (R5 boundary 'owns ONLY escalation/HANDOFF rungs ... the boundary is tested') + AC3 (R6 cite NEW-03/NEW-13 by id); NEW-03 AC6 (THE single canonical HANDOFF contract for the 5 anti-idle layers); E8-20 AC3 (COH-1 VERIFIER_ROLE default == 'verifier.md' byte-for-byte + ImportError fallback test). Live E8-14/E8-21 desc mention 'R5'/'scope contract' in PROSE only; no AC#, no boundary test spec.
(3) ZERO RELATIONS: live /relations/ for E8-14(56), E8-21(61), NEW-10(253), E4-S2(34), E4-S3(35), NEW-03(259), NEW-09(256), NEW-11(262), E8-20(60), E7-REQ11(50/516) ALL return blocking/blocked_by/relates_to/duplicate = 0. The R5 scope contract is asserted in prose with no relates_to edge between E8-14<->E8-21; NEW-14's promised blocked_by edges absent (GT-D2 sibling, do not re-report as new — cross-ref only).
(4) NEW-11 vs E7-REQ11.3 (seq 191 'pre_compact_hook.py checkpoint writer + baseline-hash producer') is a REAL overlap with NEW-11 ('PreCompact resume_state_hash checkpoint hook'); CONFIRMED but NEW-11 adds COH-2 re-add-to-spine semantics REQ11.3 lacks, so it is overlap-not-pure-dup -> relates_to, not cancel.
(5) E6-S5 (story:E6-S5) is live in DONE state for a bidirectional state-mirror story the spine (never fired, GT-1) cannot have completed -> off-Done PATCH valid.
CORRECTION (why revised, not confirmed): the finding calls 'NEW-09/256 + E8-20/60 both own execution_bounds' a created dup. REFUTED as accidental-dup: NEW-09 is a DESIGNED conditional fallback -- its live desc reads 'Conditional create. Only instantiate if UPD-E8-20 is NOT taken ... skip this create unless that fold is rejected', and it sits in BACKLOG (not Spec-Verified) while E8-20 took the ownership fold. It is a dormant either/or, not an accidental clobber. It SHOULD still be cancelled now the fold is taken, so the action survives, but the 'dup' framing is wrong. Severity stays P0: the dropped R1/R5/R6/COH-1 contracts are the load-bearing coordination semantics that prevent two stories from conflicting-editing evaluate_stop and prevent VERIFIER_ROLE drift (GT-7 fail-closed-but-stuck); losing them silently breaks the spine's coherence guarantees.

recommended_action.detail:
FIX-THE-PIPELINE (root cause, do once): patch apply_upsert_plan.py to (a) render op['acceptance_criteria'] into description_html as an

Acceptance criteria

    block appended to the existing desc, and (b) emit relation POSTs to /work-items/{id}/relations/. Then RE-APPLY so the dropped ACs persist. CONTENT EDITS (per item): UPD E8-14 -> append AC1/AC2 (R5: owns 005_run_state.sql migration + reentrancy-guard half; boundary tested); UPD E8-21 -> append AC1/AC2/AC3 (R5 escalation-rungs half + R6 cite-by-id); UPD NEW-10 -> append AC2 (R1 E4-S2/E4-S3 EXTEND not re-register); UPD NEW-03 -> append AC6 (canonical HANDOFF contract for the 5 layers); UPD E8-20 -> append AC2/AC3 (VERIFIER_ROLE byte == 'verifier.md' + ImportError fallback test). RELATIONS: add relates_to E8-14<->E8-21 (R5); add blocked_by E4-S2->NEW-10 and E4-S3->NEW-10 (R1); add relates_to NEW-03->{E8-14,E8-21,E5-S10,E6-S2,E6-S3} (R6 5-layer cite); add relates_to NEW-11->E7-REQ11 (seq 191) NOT cancel (it adds COH-2).
